E: So, what I want you to do is obvious...

C: 还是给点更实际的奖励吧…
E: How about a more concrete reward...

C: 辛苦了！我叫了增援的部队过来援救人质…嗯？现在什么情况？
E: Thanks for your help! I called reinforcements to come and help save the hostage... Hm? What's going on?

C: 你越是努力盯着黑板，那些线条便越是在你眼前模糊成一道道虚影——别问为什么人在梦中还会犯困，这大概就是知识的力量吧。
E: The harder you stare at the board, the more the lines start to blur before your eyes — don't ask why people still get tired in a dream. It's probably because of the power of knowledge.

C: 你说啥？我听不懂啊…
E: What did you say? I don't understand...

C: 缺乏目标感。
E: Lack of sense of purpose.

C: 还有半杯水呢。
E: The glass is half full.

C: 哦？有回音，看来「脑波扫描」成功了！
E: Oh? There's an echo. Looks like the Brainwave Scan was successful!

C: 也许，这也是一种对睡眠时长的「开拓」吧。
E: Maybe this is also a kind of trailblazing on the concept of sleep duration.

C: （无视它）
E: (Ignore it)

C: 不…这个计算过程，让你很想喊出「异议！」，你需要再想想。
E: No... This whole calculation process just makes you want to scream out "Objection!". You need to rally your thoughts.

C: 好痛！这狮子怎么还会咬人的…可是，到底哪里出了问题啊？
E: Owwch! Didn't expect it to bite... But, what exactly did you get wrong?

C: （无视它）
E: (Ignore it)

C: 吼，趁年轻，要像本狮这样多积累些智慧，异乡的勇士。这样再过上几万个光历年，你就能在利衡币里泡澡了吼！
E: Ho, you must accumulate a wealth of knowledge like me while your years are still ahead of you, warrior from outlands. That way, you'll be bathing in Balance Coins after tens of thousands of Light Calendar years!

C: 对富人来说，财富是可以随时间不断增加的，拥有的时间越多，能挣到的钱就越多。贫穷的人做不到这点，才会熟视无睹吼！
E: To the rich, wealth can accumulate with time. The more time you have, the more money you will make. The poor can't do this, which is why they pay no heed!

C: 答题…需要把手放进去。但是，*我们*够不到！
E: You need to... put your hand in for the answer. But, we can't reach it!

C: 卫兵阿扎。
E: The guard Aza.

C: 虽然城里有许多狮口，打坏一个也不要紧，但我还不想这么快就碎掉，求您饶我一命，我送您点礼物当做补偿吼——
E: There may be many lion's maws in the city, and destroying one would be of no consequence, but I don't want to be smashed so soon. Spare me, and I'll gift you a token as an apology...

C: 不过他们信克里珀，这好像倒也正常。
E: But they believe in Qlipoth, which makes this normal.

C: 不急着看。便签又不会自己跑掉。
E: There's no rush. It's not like the note is going to go running off on its own.

C: 还不错，这种偏僻地方也没什么可挑剔的了。呼呼，这是说好的，送给你！
E: Not bad, I can't really complain about anything since I'm out here. *crackle* This is what we agreed on, take it!

C: 最多算对了四分之一——正确答案是垃圾桶盖！
E: That's far from correct. The correct answer is a trashcan's lid!

C: 你是白痴吗？把两个7加在一起，那就是<unbreak>77</unbreak>啊！
E: Wait, are you an idiot? Obviously, it's <unbreak>77</unbreak>!

C: 变成金垃圾桶。
E: To become a gold trashcan.

C: 创造？天啊，难道你不知道我很愚钝吗？我懂了，你在嘲笑我！
E: Invent things? Oh, heavens. Don't you know I'm way too dumb for that? I get it, you're mocking me!

C: 这年头，能有你这样大志向的年轻人已经不多了，我很欣慰。
E: Youngsters with your ambition are a rare sight in this day and age. I am comforted by your presence.

C: 是了，灰头发，烧火棍，原来你就是！
E: Hm... Gray hair, flaming bat... It really is you!

C: 传授「小麦脆饼」的做法。
E: Teach them how to make "crisp wheat biscuits."

C: 这是一只小奇美拉，不知为何会出现在神像边。如果奇美拉也信仰泰坦，它会祈祷什么？
E: It's a tiny chimera, for some reason, hanging out near the statue. If chimeras could believe in the Titans, what would they pray for?

C: 叫它「麦田守望者」。
E: Let's just call it Guardian of the Wheat Field.

C: …「{NICKNAME}」！怎么样？
E: ...{NICKNAME}! How's that?

C: 放心，我早有准备。要用魔法打败魔法，用科学打败科学……
E: Fear not, I've come prepared. We'll fight magic with magic, and science with science...

C: 五…四…三…
E: Five... four... three...

C: 原来如此。
E: I see.

C: 好啦，把可爱的心思收一收，我们一起在附近走走吧。
E: Alright, tuck your lovely thoughts aside. Let's take a walk around.

C: 想做些有意义的事。
E: I want to do something meaningful.

C: 真好~有你陪着，简单的日常也变得迷人了。记得再来找我玩哦♪
E: This is nice~ Just having you here make even the ordinary feel enchanting. Come hang out with me again sometime, okay? ♪

C: 残破的盔甲被随意丢弃在一旁，表面遍布着刀剑砍伐的痕迹，但你认出了刻在头盔中央的小小的天秤标志，你记得这位忠于信念的战士。
E: The damaged suit of armor has been carelessly cast aside, covered in sword slashes. However, you recognize the insignia in the center of the helmet of a set of scales and recall the soldier who remained true to his faith.

C: 噫！纷争的领地！可我的目的地本是布利萨…难道，战矛已攻到此处了吗……
E: Eek! The land of Strife! But I was meant for Bulsa... Have the spears of war advanced this far...?

C: …什么事？我正准备出发，若是不急，就等我回来再说吧。
E: ...Yes? I'm preparing to leave — if it's not pressing, just wait for my return.

C: …布利萨？我前不久才见过他们的城主，那座城邦早就在纷争的战火中被摧毁了。现在才去往布利萨，怕是为时已晚。
E: ...Bulsa? I recently met their ruler — that city-state has already fallen to the flames of Strife. Any journey to Bulsa now would be futile.

C: …神驹复苏中。愿瑟希斯捍卫你的思想。
E: ...Divine foal awakening. May Cerces safeguard your thoughts.

C: 什么也看不见…我一脚跌进池水里，浑身都湿透了，池水很冷，实在…太冷了……
E: I can't see anything... I stumbled and fell into the pool, my whole body drenched. The water is so cold, unbearably... freezing...

C: 你怎么老喝那瓶蜜酿？
E: Why are you always drinking from that bottle of honey brew?

C: 不，一开始不是的。
E: No, at least not at the beginning.

C: （摸摸它的头顶）
E: (Pat its head)

C: 我有些事情想问你…
E: I have a few questions for you...

C: 正在此时，小友便与我搭话，你说这是巧也不巧。
E: While I was lingering on that thought, you began a conversation with me. What a pleasant coincidence.

C: 希望你能够尊重我！我在这里的行为，不仅是为了我自己的灵魂誓言，更是为了众多生灵的平安！
E: Show me some respect, please! I do what I do, not only because I swore on my soul, but also for the sake of peace!

C: 这次入侵对你造成了什么影响？
E: How did the invasion affect your life?

C: 你在思念谁？
E: Who are you thinking about?

C: 你…你怎么知道？难道说你已经听说了那个女人的故事？
E: How... How did you know that? Have you heard about the story of that woman?

C: 此言差矣。忙碌与否不是看手头上有没有正在进行的工作，而是在于头脑里是否在发生急剧的风暴。这就是我温世齐的工作哲学。
E: That's not true. How busy I am should not be judged by any existing projects, but whether any brainstorming is happening in my head. This is my work philosophy.

C: 同理，如果在休息的时候心系工作，那么这才叫真正的用心工作。你明白了吗？
E: Similarly, thinking about work during your break is what should be considered as true devotion to your work. You got it?

C: 哈——哈欠……
E: *Yawning*

C: 我……我不会喝酒。
E: I... I don't drink.

C: 让我摸摸佩佩吧！
E: Let me pet Peppy!

C: 那也这么相处过来了，不是吗？他会学会的。
E: And yet he survived, no? He'll figure it out.

C: 黑塔现在回空间站了？
E: Has Herta returned to the station?

C: 那个…歼星舰的事…
E: Um... about the starnought...

C: 才不是，我有些话想跟你说…
E: You've got the wrong idea. There's something I want to tell you...

C: 我是这里的「特别联络员」，成天要和各色各样的物种打交道，我这种大忙人，你要见我，自然得找我提前预约。
E: As the special liaison officer here, I deal with people from all walks of life daily. You need an appointment to meet someone as busy as me.

C: 遇到困难，可以喊人来帮忙。
E: You can ask for help if you run into any trouble.

C: 同为她心爱的造物，有些家伙每天就知道站着动动嘴皮子，什么事也不干。我跟它们可不一样，早点修好这里，她就早点回来，我哪有时间跟你这里慢慢磨蹭？
E: Some of Ruan Mei's creations never walk the talk and do nothing every day. I'm not like them. The sooner we fix up this place, the sooner Miss Ruan Mei will come back. So, I can't afford to while away my time with you here!

C: 但我是个成熟的造物了，我不能用这么软糯的名字，我得有气魄，这样才能让阮•梅女士对我刮目相看。
E: But now that I've matured, such a soft name doesn't fit me anymore. I need a more confident vibe to impress Miss Ruan Mei.

C: 可是「芝士流心」给大家留下的刻板印象已经无法改变，即使是本天才也没有办法改变！哇咔咔，那干脆就换个名字重新开始吧！
E: The impression "Molten Cheese Tart" has left on everyone is set too deep to be changed, not even by a genius like me! So, I decided to get a new name and start afresh!

C: 呜！
E: Woo!

C: 是{NICKNAME}吗？我正在远程观察这里的珍稀物种呢，没想到能见到你……
E: Is it {NICKNAME}? I'm remotely observing the rare species here. It's a surprise to see you...

C: 检测到视野内有生命迹象，识别中…识别成功…已自动进入闲聊模式。
E: Signs of life detected within the field of vision. Identification in progress... Identification successful... Chatting mode automatically activated.

C: 当前时间段繁忙，请勿打扰，请勿打扰。
E: Currently busy. Please do not disturb. Please do not disturb.

C: 竟然敢打着吉奥里亚的名号招摇撞骗，实在是渎神之举！
E: How dare you swindle others using Georios' name — a most blasphemous act!

C: 愿尼卡多利在你的头顶降下战矛！卫兵已经在来的路上了，等着受审吧！
E: May Nikador drop their lance over your head! The guards are already on their way. Prepare for judgment!

C: 你终于搞明白这玩意的作用——它正沉默地提醒你，三种仪式道具就在这附近。或许是被信徒们收起来了，仔细找找或许能有所发现。
E: You finally understand what this thing is used for... It is silently telling you that the three ceremonial items are nearby. Perhaps they have been hidden away by the disciples... You may be able to find something if you search carefully.

C: 啊…又一位寻求智慧的献祭者，你带来了三位泰坦的恩赐…吉奥里亚…土壤孕育万物众生…法吉娜…绵延的泉水带来生机……
E: Ah... Another follower bearing offerings in search of wisdom. You have brought the blessings of the three Titans... Georios... The soil that nurtures all living things... Phagousa... The ever-reaching springs that bring life...

C: 照你的说法，逻辑学课堂可就一文不值了，但别忘了，你是从那毕业的。
E: If that is true, logic classes would be completely worthless. Let us not forget that this was your major.

C: 若是否定「阿卡索斯」，就相当于推翻所有数学知识，智者可要考虑清楚了。
E: If we deny the existence of "Icatus," we overturn all knowledge of mathematics. This is something that you must consider carefully, wise one.

C: 居住在阿卡索斯的人们，自然对城邦有着认识，任何来到过、见过或是听说过阿卡索斯的人，也会形成对应的认识。
E: Those who live in Icatus will naturally have a cognition of it. Anyone who has been to, seen, or heard of Icatus, will help form the corresponding cognition.

C: 就是这里。我刚刚简单调查了周围，没有危险，也没有新发现。
E: Right here. I just did a basic check of the perimeter and there's nothing dangerous or any new discoveries out there.

C: 宁芙的事，你有线索吗？
E: Nympha. Got any clues about her?

C: …大家都说您喜欢开玩笑，看来是真的。
E: Everyone says you have a playful sense of humor, and it seems they're right.

C: 姐姐，你还真是五十步笑百步呢。我们两个，哪个不叫老爹操碎心？说起来，小时候还是你带着我到处惹事……
E: Guess we're both troublemakers, sis. Dad never caught a break with either of us, did he? Remember how you used to drag me into your schemes...

C: 买都买了，就先用着呗。
E: You've already bought it, so put it to use.

C: 爷好饿啊……
E: I'm so hungry...

C: 别说这种晦气话！呸呸呸！
E: Don't say such terrible things! Bah!

C: 那还蛮…蛮有设计感的。
E: That's a really... innovative way of coming up with it.

C: 不是。科可波阁下的主人从来只有一位，那便是万敌殿下。
E: No. Kokopo belongs to only one master, and that is His Highness, Mydei.

C: 原来…<color=#dbc291ff>那头大地兽的恋爱对象，就是万敌啊！</color>
E: So... <color=#dbc291ff>that dromas' true love is none other than Mydei!</color>

C: 埃德加…我…这一生过得很幸福。
E: Edgar... I... had a wonderful life.

C: 发生什么事了？
E: What happened?

C: 为什么神明要将这*不公平的命运*加诸于我的身上？
E: Why must the gods impose this unfair fate upon me?

C: 那你呢！为什么？
E: And you! Why?

C: 我们承诺向{RUBY_B#律法之泰坦}塔兰顿{RUBY_E#}的代言人支付合理报酬。
E: We promise to offer fair payment as representatives of {RUBY_B#Law Titan}Talanton{RUBY_E#}.

C: 你，听说过我的故事？
E: You— have you heard my story?

C: 阿卡索斯？印象里是早已覆灭的遥远城邦吧。
E: Icatus? If memory serves, that's a distant city-state long since fallen.

C: 是，那时她还不够瘦。
E: Right. She wasn't that slim in those days.

C: 据说，黎明机器是艾格勒赠送给刻法勒的礼物，艾格勒把许多光芒都塞进了这颗巨大的机械球里。
E: Supposedly, the Dawn Device was a gift from Aquila to Kephale — Aquila stuffed lots of radiance into that huge mechanical sphere.

C: 算了，再创世之后再相见吧。
E: Never mind, we'll meet again after the Era Nova.

C: 等你先休息一阵子，我们再制定下一步计划吧。
E: Why don't you rest a bit? We can work out our next steps afterward.

C: 你们侧耳倾听，可那并非小贩们的交谈，而是更尖锐、更密集的号叫。
E: You listen carefully, but instead of merchant chatter, you hear shrill, intense cries.

C: 我们会像驾战车一样将它们通通碾碎，对不对，{NICKNAME}？
E: We'll crush them all like riding a chariot, right, {NICKNAME}?

C: 当黑夜取代最后的黎明，侵吞一切光芒，能够破除邪恶的威胁，守护整个世界的，唯有它——「最强之矛」！
E: When the Night overtakes the last Dawn, devouring all brightness, only one thing can banish evil threats and safeguard the whole world — the Strongest Lance!

C: 哼哼，这点小插曲， 不会动摇我们的决心。
E: Heheh, that's merely a trifle. It won't sway us.

C: 是先前在神山上，天父的声音清清楚楚告诉我的。
E: Clear as day — the sky father's voice on the peak of the holy mountain.

C: 神谕确实有假的。
E: The prophecy does indeed have falsehoods.

C: 汗流浃背了，阁下夸他就好，我也没做什么。
E: Heh. You got me acting up a cold sweat here. You can just praise my friend. I didn't do much.

C: 他去了哪里作战呢？
E: Where's he stationed?

C: 我给阿波罗尼写了一封信，可以拜托你帮、帮我送给他吗？
E: I wrote a letter to Apollonius, c—could you help me deliver it to him?

C: 有瓜？你好，我吃一口。
E: Got some tea? Hey, spill it!

C: 他们是怎么认识的？
E: How did they meet?

C: 记得友爱之馆录有这雅努斯迷津的使用方法，让我回忆回忆……
E: I remember the Library of Philia has records about how to use Janus' Maze. Allow me a moment to recall...

C: 我不在乎我们是泡沫还是洋流，是真实还是虚妄……
E: I don't care if we're bubbles or ocean currents, reality or illusion...

C: 看看「疑难杂症」那页。
E: Check out the "Baffling Conundrum" page.

C: 看看别的。
E: Look at another one.

C: 救世主阁下，涅赖德斯大人，这次真是麻烦二位了。
E: Great Deliverer, Lady Nereides, your help has been absolutely invaluable to us.

C: 为了给你准备这个…礼物，虎克和老爹花了好大力气呢！
E: It took Hook and daddy a lot of effort to prepare this gift for you!

C: 电影里的机器人都好厉害哦！大家都好高、好大…还有翅膀！
E: They sounded so cool! They're really huge and they have wings!

C: 哎，我也知道这事儿肯定不可能……
E: *sigh*, I'm not sure you guys will agree, but...

C: 还是说，要再送{NICKNAME}一次礼物？！
E: {NICKNAME}, how about you give me some ideas... and I get you some more gifts?

C: 姬子小姐和我说，如果银鬃铁卫有意如此，可以随时向她请求帮助。
E: But Himeko told me that if the Silvermane Guards do go ahead with it, she'll be glad to help.

C: 尽管我相信星穹列车以及列车上其他乘客的力量，但是基础安防还是不能疏忽的。
E: I'm sure you guys and the Express itself are more than capable of maintaining security... but it's important not to overlook basic prevention methods.

C: 这就是科学的芬芳。
E: That's the smell of science happening.

C: 问呗。
E: Ask away.

C: 要不你叫几个朋友，我教你们玩「星际十三步」——聚会游戏我也很擅长。
E: Why don't you call over some friends, and I'll teach you how to play Thirteen Star-Steps - I'm good at party games too, you know.

C: 我是军人出身，也没什么照顾小孩子的经验。晴霓小的时候，我没有足够的时间陪她，又担心她一个人非常寂寞，就给她买了一大堆毛绒玩偶。
E: I come from a military background, so I don't have much experience when it comes to caring for children. When Qingni was a child, I rarely had time for her. I often worried about her feeling very lonely, so I bought her a lot of dolls.

C: 丹、丹鼎司？！
E: A—Alchemy Commission!?

C: 不会吧？你是故意骗我的。
E: You've gotta be kidding me?

C: …明明是那么幸福的梦，为什么醒来只会流出悲伤的眼泪呢？
E: ...My dreams are happy, so why do I always end up crying when they're over?

C: 咦？你怎么知道？
E: Huh? How did you know that?

C: 对于这场入侵你怎么看？
E: What do you think about this invasion?

C: 感觉你比以前有精神了。
E: It feels like you're a lot more energized than before.

C: 我还想和你聊聊。
E: I want to chat with you more.

C: 列车。
E: The Express.

C: 那个叫做「贝洛伯格」的城市，明明只是一座末日孤城，却高手如云…等以后有时间，还希望能去那里看看。
E: That city — Belobog — it seems like such a lonely place and yet it's full of masterful fighters. When I get time, I want to take a look there.

C: 打扰一个想打瞌睡的人，比打扰一个正在熟睡的人更加恶劣。
E: It's worse to disturb someone trying to sleep than wake up someone already asleep.

C: 我没有什么想问的了。
E: I don't have any other questions.

C: 那就一起走吧。
E: Then come with us.

C: 哼哼，那我可就不客气了。
E: Hehe, don't mind if I do.

C: 如果你也有兴趣的话，我们下次叫上佩拉一起去吧。就像上次的露营一样……
E: If you're interested in going sometime, we could ask Pela to come too. It'll be just like the camping trip...

C: 指望它变回以前的样子是不是不太现实？不过人总得有点梦想嘛，哈哈哈~
E: Maybe it's too unrealistic to wish it'd go back to the way it was, but it's good to have a dream, right? Hahaha~

C: 我一眼就能看出来，她对武器改装肯定特别在行！
E: I knew from the moment I saw her that she must be really good at modifying weapons!

C: {NICKNAME}，快来！我有个问题想问你——
E: {NICKNAME}, c'mere! I've got something to ask you!

C: 总之！不管房间怎么布设，有一件事我已经下定决心了——我也要搞一个等身的帕姆玩偶。
E: Regardless of the design, I've decided on one thing — a life-size Pom-Pom doll!

C: 大概是…帕姆？
E: I'm guessing it was... Pom-Pom?

C: 都是空想而已，我没那种命呀。
E: Nothing but a fantasy. I was not fated for such an existence.

C: 帕姆给了我很多奖励！
E: Pom-Pom's given me so many rewards!

C: 如果有螺丝刀就更好了，我可以把全身的螺丝拧一拧。
E: Even better if there's a screwdriver, so I can tighten all the screws on my body.

C: 其实…我也很想知道。但以客人的身份登上列车，随意乱碰物品实在太失礼了。
E: Actually... I want to know too. But as a guest on the Express, it would be impolite to touch anything.

C: 帕姆是列车长。
E: Pom-Pom is the conductor of the Express.

C: 咦？奇怪…我明明按了按钮呀，是哪里出了问题……
E: Eh? That's weird... I pressed the button already. I wonder what went wrong...

C: 记忆碎片！合成！
E: Memory fragments! Synthesize!

C: 翻看这本登记簿，仿佛正在聆听访客们的故事与心声…嗯，看到了，这是我的留言。
E: Flipping through this book is akin to personally listening to the guests' stories and their heartfelt wishes... Yes, here it is. My message.

C: 请容许我赞美你高尚的品格！
E: Please allow me to praise your noble character!

C: 查阅片刻，我发觉「纯美」正如智库中所写：祂的消失与其诞生一样神秘。
E: Upon examining it for a moment, I found that the Beauty was described as such in the data bank: THEIR disappearance and birth are equally mysterious.

C: 别自责，这份礼物我们都很喜欢！
E: Don't beat yourself up. We all love this gift!

C: 列车组的前辈身手不凡，这我早有耳闻。丹恒阁下本就是列车护卫，列车长…列车长这么可爱我不忍心！
E: I've heard that the Crew's senior members are extremely skilled. Dan Heng is already the Express' guard, and the conductor... the conductor is too cute to ask to train!

C: 繁星密布，却也稀疏……
E: The stars are numerous, and yet widely scattered...

C: 编的，这不可能！
E: Lies! That's impossible!

C: 天才的手稿散落在此，看懂手稿的人能成为天才吗？你开始浮想翩翩……
E: The genius' manuscripts are scattered here. Can one be a genius too if one can understand their contents? Your mind starts to wander...

C: 你扫视着下方，这里是整个禁闭舱段最骇人的区域，但你并不害怕，不会有什么存在比你本人更令人畏惧了。
E: You give the area below a scan. It is the most terrifying place in the entire seclusion zone, but you are not scared. Nothing can be more fearsome than yourself.

C: 听说垃圾桶里藏着身怀绝技的{F#老奶奶}{M#老爷爷}，不知道{F#她}{M#他}能不能收我为徒……
E: I heard about this mystical old master {F#granny}{M#grandpa} inside the trashcan. I wonder if {F#she}{M#he} would ever take me on as a disciple...

C: 呀——我们的颜色都是一样的！
E: Ah, our colors match!

C: 屏幕最下方一行小字吸引了你：「系统正等待唤醒，请及时更新虹膜信息。」
E: A line at the bottom of the screen catches your attention: "System is waiting to be re-activated. Please update the iris information promptly."

C: 华劳斯想和你做朋友。
E: Wallace wants to be friends with you.

C: 看来暂时是不用再补货了。
E: Looks like the inventory won't need be restocked anytime soon.

C: 你觉得这样写怎么样，会让你食欲大振吗？
E: What do you think? Doesn't this description give you an appetite?

C: 没有的事。说起来，多亏了大守护者的决策——虽然我还没资格申请去下层转悠啦——但上下层区的物资已经开始正常运输了。
E: I would never make up a story! Anyway, thanks to the Supreme Guardian's decision — even though I still don't qualify to apply to wander around the Underworld — the flow of goods between the Overworld and Underworld has already started to normalize.

C: 哎，请等一下！不会花您太长时间的。
E: Wait a minute! It won't take long.

C: 最近出版业有啥新消息吗？
E: Any news from the publishing industry recently?

C: 《雪国冒险奇谭》说不定要解禁了。不过这个消息尚未得到证实，只是有这个可能。
E: It's possible that the ban on Tale of the Winterlands will get reversed, but this hasn't been confirmed yet. It's just a possibility.

C: 你还挺有诗意。
E: You're quite a poet.

C: 别妄自菲薄了。
E: Don't put so much pressure on yourself.

C: 继续磨炼演技。
E: Work on your acting.

C: 算了，我也得学会控制自己这暴脾气……
E: Whatever. I have to learn to control my temper...

C: 最近吗？最近我读了好多书，手里的存货又要读完了…改天是不是得再去进货了呢？
E: Lately? I've been doing a lot of reading. I'm almost done reading all the books I have in stock... Maybe I'll need to get some new books in?

C: 来，您看看这份报纸——谜底就在这上边印着啦！只要您肯舍我点冬城盾……
E: Here, the answer is right in this newspaper! It's yours as long as you give me some shield...

C: 你如何评价铁卫撤离一事？
E: What's your view of the evacuation of the Guards?

C: 等银鬃铁卫正式回归下层区，这地方的未来不是肉眼可见的光明吗？
E: Once the Silvermane Guards return to the Underworld, the glorious future of this place will be obvious to all.

C: 哦，这不有消息说上下层区马上要重新开放了嘛，我最近正忙得团团转呢。
E: Well, rumor has it that the Overworld and Underworld are opening up to each other again, so I've been busy with that.

C: 嗯？原来是这样吗？
E: Hmm? I see...

C: 为什么？
E: Why?

C: 意味着你能多挣点钱！
E: It means you can make more money!

C: 难道真的是我认错了？
E: Did I?

C: 咦，{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}问我吗？我的话…最近我的「雪地跳跳糖」又吃完了。
E: Oh, do you mean me, {F#miss}{M#mister}? Me, I'm... fresh out of snow-white popping candy.

C: 你还记得诊所的路吗？
E: Do you remember the way to the clinic?

C: 我记得大矿区有很多机器人在协助矿民工作…怎么样，那些小机器人没受伤吧？
E: I remember there being lots of support robots in the Great Mine... What about them, are those little robots all okay?

C: 啊，其实是上下层解除封锁了…
E: Ah, actually the Overworld and the Underworld are being reunited...

C: 哈哈，不是我自夸，我们宾馆的下午茶是贝洛伯格的一绝。
E: Haha, I'm not trying to brag, but our afternoon tea is a specialty of Belobog.

C: 哈哈…真是辛苦你了，不过能者多劳嘛！
E: Haha... With great power comes great responsibility!

C: 不过估计一时半会也抓不到洞螈啦…要先来聊聊天吗？
E: But I don't think I can catch any olms any time soon... Do you want to chat with me?

C: 那可不——不管是机械、科研还是摇滚，我可是样样精通喔。
E: Of course! Be it mechanics, research, or rock music — I'm a genius at all of them!

C: 哈哈，我们想到一块去了。
E: Haha, great minds.

C: 嗯？怎么突然问这个？
E: Hmm? Why are you asking me that all of a sudden?

C: …啊，我突然想到一个。就前两天…那个邓恩，你认识不？就那个军阶挺高的年轻人，禁区里管事的那个——你知道他以前在乐队里做过吧？
E: ... Ah, I got one. Two days ago, Dunn... Do you know Dunn? The young man with a high rank, that's in charge of the restricted zone. You know he used to be in a band, right?

C: 如果你们在这禁区中遇到问题就告诉我，我会为你们出面解决的。
E: If you get into any trouble around here, let me know and I'll remedy it for you.

C: 哈哈，请多关照。
E: Haha, good to see you.

C: 听起来很危险。
E: That sounds dangerous.

C: 你能再多说说工作的细节吗？
E: Can you tell me the details about your job?

C: 希露瓦当然是我的好闺蜜啦，我们还一起玩乐队呢。她是吉他手兼主唱，至于我嘛…其实是鼓手。
E: Serval is, of course, my best friend. We've even formed a band together. She's the guitarist and vocalist, while I'm the drummer.

C: 「源代码」？
E: The Source Code?

C: 怎么样，很让人心动吧？快把你的伙伴也叫上，一起加入鼹鼠党吧！
E: How's that? Are you psyched? Get your friends and join The Moles now!

C: 捂住嘴屏住息不发出声响，心停跳快窒息受不了煎熬
E: Don't make a sound hold your breath till it's over, heart pounding don't let the panic take over

C: 屋子里仅剩的一块面包，心情似霉点，扩散发酵
E: No more food left in the cupboard, the mood is dire

C: 但生活的艰难只有妈妈知道
E: Life can be so tough, only ya momma really ever knows

C: 哎~在世界中心等你回来~
E: Hey! Waiting in the center of the world for your return~

C: 不打扰你科考了。
E: Then I'll leave you to your research.

C: 欸，你来了啊——你也是来雪原里探险的吗？
E: Oh, you're here, too? Are you here on an expedition just like me?

C: 托帕是个怎样的人？
E: What do you make of Topaz?

C: 这...不好吧，这可是商业机密。
E: Hey, you see... it's a trade secret.

C: 虽然我在之后也玩了其他不少游戏，其中也不乏一线厂商的各种工业化大制作，但陪伴我最久的反而是这样一款略显简陋的游戏。
E: I've tried many other games since then, including blockbusters from top-notch developers, but Aetherium Wars, despite its somewhat dated graphics, has stuck with me the longest.
Uh...

C: 是不是很酷？来，试试看！
E: Cool, huh? C'mon, give it a try.

C: 你放心吧，准没问题。
E: Don't worry. It'll be fine.

C: 大守护者好像要颁布新政策了。
E: I think the Supreme Guardian is going to issue a new policy.

C: 个大体肥，壳薄肉鲜！
E: Plump and juicy meat, with a thin shell!

C: …谁半夜三点打电话啊？
E: ...Who calls at 3 AM?

C: 闲暇时光，靠它陪伴！
E: Use your leisure time to keep yourself company!

C: 又上哪儿调皮去了？
E: Where's that cheeky kid gone off to now?

C: 成交。
E: Deal.

C: 刺拳！刺拳！勾拳！
E: Jab! Jab! Uppercut!

C: 那为什么不接回来？
E: Then why don't you reconnect the wires?

C: 不过，我们还是把能收集来的文物全都翻新了一遍，又把它们送进展柜里。也正因此，这座博物馆看起来还算是过得去。
E: However, we still collected everything we can and restored them so that we can put them on display. Because of that, the museum is at least somewhat decent.

C: 慈祥的老人向你报以和善的微笑。
E: The kind old man smiles affably at you.

C: 呵呵…是啊。
E: Oh... yes.

C: 这是…《雪国冒险奇谭》？
E: This is... Tale of the Winterlands?

C: 我想了解你的工作。
E: I want to know more about what you do.

C: 我并不擅长与人在智慧上交锋，那种感觉就像是躺在案板上任由持着砍刀的屠夫随意宰割…相信我，你不会喜欢那种感觉的。
E: I'm not good at battles of wits. Those make me feel like I'm on a chopping board waiting to be butchered by whoever has the knife... Trust me, it's not a nice feeling to have.

C: 在说什么怪话…
E: What exactly are you talking about...

C: 这次「创意星期四」应该推出哪些菜品呢…咱得看看…大肠刺身、腰子炖汤、红糖芝麻酱、清蒸野牛皮、炭烤黑驴蹄、拔丝臭豆腐……
E: Let's have a look then. What shall we have for this week's Odd-taste Thursday... Gizzard sashimi, kidney stew, brown sugar sesame porridge, steamed boar skin, charcoal barbecued black donkey hooves, caramel-drizzled stinky tofu...

C: 唱什么歌呢？
E: What song are you singing?

C: 「规范」说的是仙舟诗依托于语言的特殊韵律，只要将符合韵律的排列组合全部列出来，就能确定「规范」的边界。
E: The rules refer to the special rhythm upon which Xianzhou poetry relies. As long as all the different permutations that conform to the rhythm can be exhaustively listed out, then we can define the limits of this rule.

C: 清风明月…捅我心？清风明月…踹我心？清风明月…掐我心？
E: The breeze and the moon... "poke" my heart? The breeze and the moon... "stomp" my heart? The breeze and the moon... "choke" my heart?

C: 是啊，危机总算过去了。对了，我最近从建木危机一事上得到了一些灵感，写了几句诗，你听听——
E: Indeed, everything is finally over. By the way, I have written a poem with inspiration from the Ambrosial Arbor Crisis. Please listen...

C: 我很好奇…
E: I'm curious...

C: 真的吗？
E: Really?

C: 你平时也会向蘑菇搭话吗？
E: Do you often talk to mushrooms?

C: …如果每个人都像你这么想，托蝶幻境这门艺术就要断绝了！如果当年子润老师听了制片人的这些所谓「商业考量」，《外婆桥》根本都不会问世！
E: ...If everyone thought like you did, the art of Butterfly Immersia would be wiped out! If master Zi Run had listened to these so-called "business interests" back then, "The Bridge to Grandma's Home" would've never even been released!

C: 托蝶幻境是什么？
E: What's Butterfly Immersia all about?

C: 哈哈，看来您确实不太了解我们集团。犯人也是人啊，有人的地方就有需求，有需求的地方就有生意。我们集团的使命就是让幽囚狱没有难做的生意。
E: Haha, looks like you're not familiar with our group. Prisoners are people too. Where there are people, there would be needs, and needs drive business. Our group's mission is to ensure you can do every sort of business in The Shackling Prison.

C: 后来有一次，猎群出去劫掠商队时，我爹就把我塞进了兽舰，想让我「手上沾点血，成为一个真正的男人！」
E: One day while the pack was out looting a caravan, my father forced me onto a beast ship so I would "stain my hands with blood and become a real man!"

C: 当然，我不觉得时间能解决一切问题，如果他自己不做出改变，那么他要面对的问题是不会有改观的。
E: Of course, I don't think time alone is enough to solve every problem. If he doesn't make changes for himself, then the problems he's facing will never change either.

C: 不过我相信只要我们集团内部团结一心，持续为客户提供优质的服务，就肯定能挺过这个寒冬！
E: But I believe that as long as our group stays united and continues to provide premium service to our customers, we'll definitely be able to brave through these harsh circumstances!

C: 其实嘛，很简单——说好听点是执行，说难听点是打杂；有活我们扛，有腿我们跑，拿的还是最低的工资……
E: It's really simple, actually. Diplomatically, we say we carry out executive orders. But really, it's just a bunch of menial tasks. We slog through chores, run errands, and get paid the lowest rank of wages...

C: 彦卿原本也有一双漂亮的尖耳朵…可惜早年间神策府爆发鼠患，彦卿一日正在午休，竟被硕鼠啃食了双耳的尖端……
E: Yanqing used to have a pair of beautiful pointy ears... but one day, a rat infestation broke out at the Seat of Divine Foresight during Yanqing's afternoon nap. That was when a giant rat nibbled his ears' pointy ends away...

C: 对将军来说，只要府内事务运转照旧，那便能证明我们已经履职；执勤时偶尔交谈也无伤大雅，将军于是睁一只眼闭一只眼……
E: As long as we keep operations running smoothly, then that's proof enough for him that we're fulfilling our duties. So chattering on the job is fine, and the general will generally turn a blind eye to it...

C: 哎…这么一想，咱们姐俩还真是像啊。
E: *sigh* Well, when you put it like that, I guess we are pretty similar after all.

C: 总感觉自己昨天还在阳间蹉跎人生，怎么一眨眼就在这幽囚深狱工作几百年了呢？
E: I feel like I was still living in the mortal realm just yesterday. How it is that hundred of years can pass by in a blink while I work here?

C: 想跟你打听一些事。
E: I'd like to ask you about something.

C: 你认识一位带着棺木的行商吗？
E: Do you know of a traveling merchant with a coffin?

C: 这…一言难尽啊。
E: It's... complicated.

C: 永巷滔滔不绝地描述着你在幽囚狱的英勇战斗。其中的很多细节你自己都不记得，搞不好是他乱编的。
E: Yongxiang continues to describe your valiant battle in The Shackling Prison. Included are a great many details that even you don't remember. You suspect he might be making some of this up.

C: 哎，这么点钱怎么够花啊，再不开工的话，家用怎么办……
E: Oh, how is this enough money for the whole family? We'll go broke if I don't get work soon...

C: 卜算，依旧是卜算。具体内容，请恕我不能透露。
E: Divinations. Still more divinations. Please forgive me for not divulging any further details.

C: 我不好说…
E: I can't say for sure...

C: 工造司怎么会派你来这儿？
E: Why did the Artisanship Commission send you over here?

C: 举个例子，我平时负些检修工作，可和我对接的几位卜官各个都有颗玲珑心，说话恨不得绕几百个圈子。
E: For example, I normally do maintenance work here, but the managing diviners I interact with are all so crafty, that they just beat around the bush all day without making anything clear.

C: 但他，的的确确是一个值得我骄傲的儿子。
E: However, he was indeed a son that I was proud of.

C: 如需…哦，是你呀，你有什么事吗？
E: If you need— Oh, it's you. What's up?

C: 驾驭星槎，逐潮来去，检视那些珍珠般的持明卵，看着他们在沉睡中发光，真是世间最美妙的景象了。
E: It's the most wondrous sight in the cosmos to ride across the tides on a starskiff, marveling at those pearly Vidyadhara eggs as they glow in hibernation.

C: 我会允许他们把这些东西暂时寄放在我这儿。
E: I let them stow items with me while the rebirth happens.

C: 我现在负责打理穷观阵的一处阵法，至于具体的内容…无非是推衍卜算，记录涨落幅度之类的事务。
E: I'm now in charge of a matrix at the Matrix of Prescience... My jobs include making divination inferences and recording the changes.

C: 至于这玩意从哪来的…可能是狱卒们废弃的吧，反正我找到的时候它已经破成一坨废铁了，为了修好它废了不少力气。
E: As for where I got this thing from... I imagine one of the prison guards chucked it out. Either way, when I found it, it was just a pile of scrap metal. I had to put a lot of work into fixing it.

C: 今日我心神不宁、集中不了注意力…这实在是不妙。
E: I feel restless and distracted today... This is bad.

C: 我是星际和平公司的商业规划专家，这次随团参加公司在金人巷相关的事务。如果有任何业务咨询和合作意向，请随时和我联系。
E: I'm an IPC business planning specialist, and I'm here on business affairs related to Aurum Alley. Feel free to contact me if you have any business inquiries or are interested in cooperating with us.

C: 我听说公司有些业务员急功近利，耍弄小手段，真丢人。
E: I heard that some of our sales representatives are resorting to shady tactics to get what they want. What a disgrace.

C: 我还有事，先走了。
E: I'm busy, so I'll be heading off.

C: 小吃不错，仅止于此。
E: The snacks here are great, and that's all there is.

C: 商会？你也是金人巷商会的吗？
E: Merchant guild? Are you from the Aurum Alley Merchant Guild, too?

C: 这说明你没有听到什么不该听的内容。
E: If you're asking that question, then it means you haven't heard anything you shouldn't have.

C: 有点名气，但不多——那种眼皮子浅只知道购物和打卡拍照的小年轻肯定不知道。但在我们这种懂行的观光客圈子里，倒算是有名。
E: It's relatively famous, but not by that much, and especially not for the superficial kids who only care about shopping and taking photos. However, it's a great attraction among seasoned travelers like us.

C: 这个简单，无非是多花点时间和心思。你去哪儿玩，就和本地人多聊聊，然后去吃他们常去的本地餐厅。时间上要是赶得及，那就看看能不能赶上当地的节日和庆典活动。
E: It's not that hard. All you need is some time and dedication. Make sure to talk to the locals and eat at their favorite restaurants. And if you're lucky, attend local festivals and carnivals.

C: 抛开技术日新月异的新产品不谈，即使是些锅碗瓢盆的寻常物件，它们很少有能够长存的，修修补补也都是徒劳。
E: Forget about all the hot new tech that comes out every week. Even mundane stuff like pots and pans rarely last forever. You can fix them, but they'll soon break again.

C: 您有所不知，我这店是瞧着不咋地，那是因为我家不像某些星槎海的大店，净玩些虚的。
E: Well, well. My store may seem lackluster at first glance, but it's because I don't spend all my budget on fluffy marketing unlike those popular stores in Starskiff Haven.

C: 托您的福，还算可以吧…嘿，这金人巷复兴以后，倒也谈不上有多么大的变化，但生意确实是越来越好做了。
E: Thanks to you, it's not bad... Nothing much has changed since the revival of the Aurum Alley, but my business is thriving.

C: 处理金人巷事务
E: Take care of the affairs in Aurum Alley

C: 我店里炮制的药丸主打一个「方便易保存」，有客人看我气色好，以为我有什么调养的药物让我推荐…其实我只是精神气好，皮肤就跟着好了。
E: The pills I make are known for being easy to consume and store. Some customers ask me to recommend them some supplements after seeing my flawless skin... Well, my skin is in good condition just because I'm in a good space mentally.

C: 请恕我拒绝。
E: Then please allow me to refuse.

C: 我没什么想问的了。
E: I don't have any more questions.

C: 你们平时在哪里演出？
E: Where do you usually perform?

C: 你大概还不认识我，容我自我介绍一下吧！我以前是「药王秘传」的一名莳者，现在是阿合马铁窗集团的首席技术研发官。
E: You probably don't know who I am. Let me introduce myself. I'm a former disciple of Sanctus Medicus and the current Chief Technical R&D Officer of the Arma Correctional Group.

C: 这东西在你手里只是普通的零食，在我们手里可是一般等价物啊！我都知道，你随随便便就能合成几千个奇巧零食！换句话说，你可是掌握了幽囚狱金融体系的发币权啊！
E: Those are just boring old snacks to you, but I'll have you know that they're no different from money to us! I also know that you can synthesize thousands of Trick Snacks with a flick of your wrist... In other words, you hold the reins of The Shackling Prison's entire financial system!

C: 我的产品可都是我亲自试验过的，绝对有品质保障！当然，你要是吃坏肚子了，我这还有解毒止泻的药，免费供应，嘿嘿～
E: I personally try out all my products, so rest assured I can attest to their quality! Of course, if your stomach does become upset, I can supply you with detoxification and anti-diarrheal medication on the house, hehe.

C: 也许最后摆在我们面前的结论和我们内心所猜想的最坏可能一样…但我宁愿再尝试一次。
E: Perhaps the worst-case scenario might turn out to be the truth, but I'm willing to give it a shot.

C: 推测：因为仙舟联盟的有机体拥有远优于「我们」的计算机科技，所以他们可以在「我们」展露敌意的瞬间，就将「我们」囚禁。
E: Conjecture: The Xianzhou Alliance possesses computational technology far superior to ours. Consequently, these intelligent organic lifeforms were able to incarcerate us the moment we displayed hostility.

C: 总之，这五行调谐、生克有序，便是健康的证明。外加烟熏食物自带祛湿属性，实在是养生快餐的不二之选！
E: Anyway, the balanced presence and stable order of the five elements in the beef will bring about health. The fact that smoked food can remove extra moisture in the body makes the dish a healthy fast food option!

C: 就先聊到这儿吧。
E: Let's call it a day for now.

C: {NICKNAME}，眼下幽囚狱仍在重建秩序，谈不上安全，请不要四处闲逛。
E: {NICKNAME}, the Shackling Prison is still trying to have peace and order restored. It is not safe, so please try not to wander around.

C: 我想想…有个几年了吧？哎，仙舟人的时间意识太薄弱了，动辄几十一百年的，搞得我也有些糊涂了。
E: Let me think... A few years? Xianzhou people have no sense of time, they all talk about decades and centuries and it's got me all confused.

C: 呃…虽然我也不知道，为啥这个搞灵异怪谈的账号，现在成了破除迷信的大使……
E: Uhh... I'm not really sure why an account that deals with the occult has now become the embassy of myth-busting though...

C: 嗯嗯，下次再聊～有事没事记得来金人巷找我玩啊！没空来也没关系，记得给我直播间打赏也行，嘻嘻～
E: Yeah, another time~ Remember to come back and see me in Aurum Alley whenever! If you don't get time, just send me a tip on my live stream, hehe~

C: 我该走了。
E: I've got to go.

C: 不过，我是冥差里最可爱的那个，大人您请记好了。
E: However, I am the cutest spiritfarer of all, don't you forget that!

C: {NICKNAME}大人虽是化外民，可没少和十王司打交道…您认识雪衣大人、寒鸦大人、还认识刚上任的藿藿大人，见过几个冥差也不稀奇。
E: Though you are an Outworlder, {NICKNAME}, you have had many dealings with the Ten-Lords Commission... You know Madam Xueyi, Hanya, and the newly appointed Huohuo. Having met a few spiritfarers along the way is to be expected.

C: 你为什么来十王司工作？
E: Why are you working at the Ten-Lords Commission?

C: 你一直在十王司工作吗？
E: Have you been working at the Ten-Lords Commission for a long time?

C: 「一者，令堕长生。二者，贪取不死。三者，迷乱心智。四者，诱陷魔阴。五者，残杀胞族。」
E: "One, Involuntary Immortalization. Two, Immortality Theft. Three, Beguilement. Four, Mara Ensnarement. Five, Phratry Murder."

C: 不订了，有没有普通成衣？
E: I'll pass. Do you have ordinary ready-made clothes?

C: 「十王」究竟是谁？
E: Who exactly are the Ten Lords?

C: …不重要了，我们姐妹曾因魔阴之孽犯下极大罪过，深自懊悔。得蒙十王垂怜，我为自己和姐姐换来一次驻留还阳的机会。
E: ...Never mind about that. We sisters had been stricken by mara and committed a great offense. We were regretful. Thankfully, the Ten Lords took pity on us and granted us another chance.

C: 我跟你说，向帝弓祈祷是真的有用，你看，建木的风波这就停了下来，也不见它再次生发了…这就好，这就好…我还想再过点安生日子呢！
E: You know what? Praying to the Reignbow really works. See how the Ambrosial Arbor crisis got sorted out and the tree stopped growing? Thank goodness, thank goodness... I want to enjoy my peaceful life again!

C: 亲亲，这是打算挑一款玉兆自己用，还是送人呢？我这边比较推荐的是这款最新型号的「瑞兆<unbreak>15</unbreak>」，您要看看吗？
E: Babe, are you picking out a jade abacus for yourself, or as a gift? My recommendation is the latest model: The Auspicious XV. Wanna take a look?

C: 我看亲亲很感兴趣的样子，如果是第一次买玉兆，或者说只是想换新，体验最前端的玉兆科技，那么买「瑞兆<unbreak>15</unbreak>」准没有问题。
E: I can tell you're very interested in this, babe. If you're buying your first jade abacus or upgrading to a new one to experience the latest in cutting-edge tech, then the Auspicious XV is a great choice.

C: 对顾客，小店都是一视同仁的。对不是顾客的人，那小店也不必用接待客人的方式对待。
E: If you're a customer, I treat all of you equally. Otherwise, I am not obligated to treat you as such.

C: 建木危机闹得可真大……
E: The Ambrosial Arbor Crisis has blown up big time...

C: 再后来，我在县里的威望越来越强，甚至算是实质上的地方统治者。有一年，那个帝国的皇帝还来找我，求我给他的长子治病。这算是我的高光时刻了。
E: Later on, my reputation in the county rose to new heights, to the point where I was the undisputed local governor. There was even a year when the land's Emperor sought me out to treat his eldest son. That was the highlight of my time there.

C: 你把这马褂儿给我脱下来吧！
E: Get your story straight!

C: 哎！你不是说你不要了吗？你别急啊。那石狮子是神策府的石狮子，有灵性，天冷一刮风，自己就知道换个地儿取暖了，所以它来咱星槎海了嘛。
E: Hey! I thought you said you didn't want it! Simmer down, that stone lion belongs to the Seat of Divine Foresight, so it's sentient. When it gets cold from the bellowing winds, it knows how to find a warmer place. That's why it came to Starskiff Haven.

C: 建木到底是什么材料做的？
E: What exactly is the Ambrosial Arbor made of?

C: 封建迷信要不得！
E: Let's not be superstitious!

C: 你怎么还能睡的着呢？
E: How could you even have fallen asleep?

C: 再见！咱继续去找神仙姐姐啦，{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}也一定能在仙舟上实现愿望的！
E: Bye-bye! I need to keep searching for the celestial lady. {F#Miss}{M#Mister}, your dreams will certainly come true as well in Xianzhou!

C: 讲座的名字我都想好了，就叫《檀檀谈谈》吧，或者叫《檀檀讲坛》！你觉得如何？
E: I've come up with a name for it already: Tantan's Tenet! Or In Tandem With Tantan. What do you think?

C: 嗯，时间也来得及，丹鼎司的出口药品分级相关的资料…需要找到对应的负责人确认。另外…商会的伴手礼订单，应该到货了，待会儿得派人去取。
E: Mm, there's still time. The relevant documentation for the Alchemy Commission's medicinal exports... requires confirmation from the person in charge. Also... souvenir bag orders for the merchant guilds should have arrived. I'll send someone to pick them up later.

C: 你从小就这么好看吗？
E: Have you always been this pretty?

C: 「灯笼火树，争然九陌之时，舞席歌筵，大启千灯之夜」，办得真好啊……
E: "Sea of fiery lights illuminate the countless suburbs, while feasts and songs unveil nights of innumerable glows"... Nicely done.

C: 我记得你。
E: I remember you.

C: 如果你腿脚灵活，我会在检票的时候拼死抱住检票员，让你强冲安检。
E: For example, if you're nimble, then I'll hold onto the ticket inspector for dear life during the ticket inspection while you rush through the security check.

C: 暂时不需要。
E: Not at the moment.

C: 我真的是狐人！
E: I really am a foxian!

C: 至于要饭的吉祥话，就更是难了，见了公司的人说「愿琥珀王保佑你」，见了博识学会的学者要说「愿博识尊保佑你」，啥都分不清的，就帝弓保佑。
E: As for begging taglines, that's even harder. If you see an IPC fellow, you have to say, "May the Amber Lord protect you." If it's an Intelligentsia Guild scholar, it's "May Nous guide you." If you can't really tell, the Reignbow will bless them.

C: 没事儿，下次我也在这儿等您——愿帝弓保佑你！
E: All good, I'll be waiting here for you. May the Reignbow bless you!

C: 怎么样，是不是很酷？
E: How about that! Isn't it cool?

C: 我早知道了…
E: I already knew that...

C: 坦白说，我不知道。
E: Honestly? I don't know.

C: 这里真无聊，连个说话的人都没有，你既然来了，要不要陪我玩玩。
E: It is so boring in here. Not even anybody to talk to. Since you're here already, do you want to play with me for a bit?

C: 你谁？
E: Who are you again?

C: 岂止仙舟人，长生种的欲望全都难以消化。这些家伙……
E: Not just Xianzhou humans, but the desires of all long-life species are tough to digest. These people...

C: 我怕死了…特别是尾巴大爷叫我的时候，我已经想好下辈子重生的事了……
E: I'm scared to death... especially when Mr. Tail called me. I had already started planning out my post-rebirth life...

C: 没关系，我会自己想象。
E: That's alright. I'll just imagine them myself.

C: 尽管我对天外世界充满了期望，但是我还没有做好准备，要是就这样出发，肯定还会像上次一样中途夭折。
E: Despite being brimming with expectations of the worlds beyond the sky, I'm not ready yet. If I go off like this, I'll definitely run into a crisis midway just like last time.

C: 很好，尽情释放招式！
E: Very good, unleash your best moves all over me!

C: 很好，很好学。
E: I'm pleased to see you have a thirst for knowledge.

C: 好久没遇这种大事了…
E: It's been a long time since I ran into something this serious...

C: 这预示着大难临头啊！
E: This heralds great misfortune!

C: 我不看书的，你别问我。
E: I don't read. Don't ask me.

C: 打弹珠！打弹珠！
E: Let's play Marbles!

C: 哎，很担心丹鼎司的未来。
E: Alas, I'm very worried about the future of the Alchemy Commission.

C: 呃，这是我爸的原话！
E: Um, that was what my father said!

C: 年纪轻轻的叹什么气？
E: Why are you sighing at such a young age?

C: 吾如今返璞归真，不再是手持枪矛的女武神，亦非明镜高悬的融合神，仅是一位平凡的女童。
E: I have now returned to my original, pure state. I am no longer a warrior goddess wielding a spear, nor the all-seeing Theos Synthetos. I am but an ordinary young girl.

C: 没有任何东西能让你获得智慧，除了书籍和教训。
E: Nothing can grant you wisdom except readables and lessons.

C: 谢谢你的祝愿，相信所有人都会永远幸福。
E: Thank you for your wishes, I believe everyone will be happy forever.

C: 别忘了我们啊，灰子！
E: Don't forget about us, Gray Mystery!

C: …小灰鱼实在好奇的话，不如与我共同觅食一遭？或许，你能亲眼看到答案呢。
E: ...Little Gray Fry, if you're that curious, why not join me for a hunt? Perhaps you'll find the answer with your own eyes.

C: 没有任何东西能让你获得智慧，除了「我们对智慧还一无所知」的认知。
E: Nothing can grant you wisdom except the realization that "we know nothing of wisdom at all."

C: {NICKNAME}阁下，请你收下这副花冠。
E: Please accept this flower crown, {NICKNAME}.

C: 嘁，你这只知道死磕的莽夫，要是听我的，别死守那些沦陷在即的城邦，少说还能延长一千年的黎明！
E: Tch, says the brute who only knows how to butt heads! If you'd just listened to me instead of desperately clinging to those doomed city-states, we could've extended the Dawn by at least a thousand years!

C: 是我。
E: Yes, that's me.

C: 是啊，这相声可是冒犯的艺术，理不歪，笑不来。
E: That's right. Crosstalk is the art of offending others! It wouldn't be funny if it makes too much sense.

C: 内功啊…我觉得这估计很难。
E: Inner cultivation... I think that'll be really difficult.

C: 温泉随时都可以泡，这么刺激的比赛错过了就没有了啊！
E: You can go visit the hot springs any time, but missing such an exciting martial competition means you'll miss it forever!

C: 这个更不行啊！
E: That would be impossible!

C: 这明明都是老模具，重涂换色就当限定了？还有这个战损…
E: It's just an old model that you've repainted, and you're masquerading it as a limited item? And this "wounded version"...

C: 老夫七百有六。
E: I am <unbreak>706</unbreak> years old.

C: 没搞头。
E: Nah.

C: 作者以自己为载体，浑身散发着蓬勃的艺术朝气
E: The artist has used their own body as the receptacle, exuding a dense artistic vitality.

C: 海那边的敌人。
E: An enemy from overseas.

C: 我是～猿猴大盗！
E: I am~ The great ape thief!

C: 材质：囚犯，武器
E: Materials: Convict, weapons

C: 剪蓝色的线。
E: Cut the blue wire.

C: 豆知识，苏乐达™早些年为了抢占仙舟饮料市场，曾用过「好梦气泡饮」的注册名，但显然它不如「苏乐达」朗朗上口。
E: Trivia: In the earlier years, SoulGlad™ once used the registered name "Sweet Dreams Soda" to try to capture Xianzhou's beverage market, but it was obviously not as catchy as SoulGlad.

C: 真理就在射程范围之内，这是寰宇皆通的准则。
E: Truth lies within shot range, this is a universal principle.

C: 没事，等你有空的时候。
E: It's fine, whenever you're free.

C: 要有格局！格局！
E: You gotta think bigger!

C: 卢卡复活了！可喜可贺！
E: Luka back in! What a joyous occasion!

C: 「红梅芳喜芝兰茂，琴瑟和弦奏笙箫——来红梅佳缘，寻神仙伴侣。」
E: "A life lived in love will never be dull. Come to Bonds of Love to find your perfect match!"

C: 曾有人因随意触动穹仪导致洞天重力失常，令一半罗浮人罹患航天综合征——人们都说那是一场医学奇迹。
E: Someone once touched the Meridian Pin and caused a gravity anomaly in the delve. Because of that, half of the Luofu population suffered from spaceflight syndrome — a situation regarded by many as a miracle in the medical field.

C: 你信手抽出其中一枚卷轴。展开时，一个丝绢质感的合成声音自你脑内成形。
E: You pull out a random scroll. When you open it, a synthetic voice as smooth as silk forms in your mind.

C: 「作为纳努克座下七位大君之一，『焚风』痴迷于事物在毁灭瞬间所爆发的力量与美感。他所拥有的力量，也足以让他取代千军万马，对一个世界下达死亡判决。」
E: "As one of the Seven Ravagers, Zephyro is obsessed with the power and beauty of the moment of destruction. Being as powerful as an army, he is capable of destroying worlds at will."

C: 加油！我会为你们应援！
E: Keep it up! I'm cheering you on!

C: 还是算了吧。
E: Never mind.

C: 你摇了摇头，任这只快递盒孤零零地留在原地，望着你远去的背影。
E: You shake your head, leaving the package in place as you turn and leave.

C: 一只被打开的快递盒，就这么搁在路边。半掩的箱盖下有一叠厚厚的纸在风里微微晃动。
E: There is a courier package left open on the roadside. Under the half-opened lid is a thick stack of papers that flips gently in the breeze.

C: 这团可怜的信纸显然承受了太多的愤怒。
E: Obviously, the crumpled letter was the victim of someone's fury.

C: 打开看看。
E: Open it.

C: 这页介绍了杂技。
E: This page introduces acrobatics.

C: 你轻轻敲了敲持明的卵壳，它焦急地震动，卵壳上的鳞片微微翕张，向你示警，想让你离开此处。
E: You gently tap on the shell of the Vidyadhara egg. It shakes nervously, and the scales on the shell open and close slightly to warn and drive you away.

C: 它是鳞渊境中负责戍守「建木」的卫士之一，数百年来，古木寂静无声，恍如沉睡。它与战友们日日测算，百无聊赖。但谁又能想到建木会在那一日醒来呢？
E: It used to be one of the guards in charge of watching the Ambrosial Arbor in Scalegorge Waterscape. For centuries, the ancient tree had been quiet as though it was in a deep slumber. Taking measurements of the tree every day with its comrades bored it to death. Who would have thought that the Ambrosial Arbor would wake up on that fateful day?

C: 形虎拳馆将「延年益寿」作为卖点吸引化外民，这是否有一些欺诈的成分？
E: Tiger Boxing Gym uses "promoting longevity" as a selling point to attract outworlders. Is there a hint of fraud in this?

C: 当然你可以做题，你甚至可以全部写完。不过，你总得证明一下自己*有实力*完成所有的习题。请听题——
E: Of course. You can try to answer the questions. You could even answer all of them. However, you need to prove that you have the CAPACITY to complete all the questions. Question one...

C: 拆开看看！
E: Open it up!

C: 你依次拿起三个箱子，逐一比较它们的重量。写着「帝弓遗物」的那个箱子出奇得重，重到远远超出了你理解中「主观认知带来的偏差」。
E: You pick up the three boxes in turn, comparing their weight. The box with "Reignbow's Relic" written on it is far heavier than expected. That's a much larger weight difference than you anticipated from a "deviation caused by subjective perception."

C: 你是被骗了啊，兄弟。
E: ...you got cheated, bud.

C: 声音很虚弱，像是随时会断气那般。并且，你清楚记得你没有敲门。
E: Their voice is very weak, like they could take their last breath at any moment. You remember clearly that you never actually knocked on the door.

C: 就像开盲盒——你永远也不会知道，在打开一个垃圾桶时，里面的房客此时究竟是何种心情。
E: It's like opening a loot box. You'll never know how the tenant inside is feeling when you open the trashcan.

C: 苏乐达是梦境中最为常见的饮料——当然，还是得花钱购买。
E: SoulGlad is the most common dream in the Dreamscape — you still have to pay for it though.

C: 坚固的箱子保护着稚子最为重要的秘宝。
E: A sturdy chest that protects a child's most important treasure.

C: 一股浓郁的咖啡味从屋内传来…这可不方便入梦啊。
E: A rich aroma of coffee wafts from within the room... It seems to be a bad time for a dream.

C: 再狂野的想象也需要制约，这便是家族监管局的职责。
E: Even the wildest of imaginations must be curbed — that is the duty of The Family's surveillance agency.

C: 而后你的一切去伪存真，与古老之梦共存。
E: Where you will shed your falsities and embrace the truth, co-existing with the ancient dream.

C: 这里是家族的安全区，可以抵御忆域迷因的袭击。
E: This is The Family's safe zone that can fend off Memory Zone Meme attacks.

C: 柜门后是梦境最为隐蔽的空间，非常适合躲藏——但时间久了还是会被忆域迷因抓出来。
E: Behind the cabinet door is the Dreamscape's most hidden space, very good for hiding. But even after a while, one would still be caught and dragged away by the Memory Zone Memes.

C: 舒适的皮革沙发。可惜这里缺少能让它实现存在价值的对象。
E: A comfortable leather sofa. Unfortunately, it lacks an object that would help it realize its worth.

C: 真神奇…不知道为什么，在注视着你的时候我竟莫名感到平静……
E: How amazing... For some reason, I feel sense of tranquility when I look at you...

C: 你能明白！你是我最好的朋友！我要把所有的证书和奖章…都给你看！
E: You get it! You're my bestie! I'm gonna show all my certificates and medals here... to you!

C: 您要是宾客的话，会知道些什么吗？
E: If you are a guest, what would you like to know?

C: 作为无名客的奇妙经历或许很有趣。但不巧，我手头没有苏乐达，不然就可以安心听您讲故事了。
E: The wondrous sojourn of a Nameless could perhaps be interesting. Alas, I have no SoulGlad with me — an essential pairing when listening to your tale.

C: 最重要的…您并不信任家族，对这个梦抱有疑虑。
E: Most importantly... You don't trust The Family, and harbor disbelief in the dream.

C: 感谢您支持我的事业…这点礼物不成心意，还请您收下。
E: Thank you for supporting my work... This token gift may not be much, but please take it.

C: 抱歉，下次不会了。
E: Sorry, I won't do it next time.

C: 我当然敢。
E: Of course, I will.

C: 切诺对你露出了鄙夷的眼神，仿佛在说：「你怎么还在？」
E: Cielo flashes a disgusted look at you as if to say, "What are you still doing here?"

C: 只是…他无法忍受我的摇摆不定，最终选择了离开。呵，这就是…门卫的宿命吧。
E: I just know... that he couldn't stand all my dithering about and in the end chose to leave. *sigh* This is... the fate of all guards.

C: 你悄声走上前，很好，熊崽没有跑开，仍旧用无辜的眼神望着你。你已经开始想象那柔软的皮毛在掌心中的触感了，你相信那就是你始终在追寻的幸福。
E: You walk forward, quietly. Good: It hasn't run off but is still watching you with those innocent eyes. You begin to imagine the soft touch of that fur in the palm of your hand and wonder: Is this the happiness that you've been pursuing all along?

C: 顺从心中的欲念。
E: Submit to your heart's desire.

C: 难道你最开始就没有注意到，眼前这个生物头顶挂着的名字根本不是「冰原熊幼崽」么？
E: Didn't you notice that this whole time, the sign hanging above the creature doesn't say "Arctic Bear Cub" at all?

C: 怎么回事？在你校准对方情绪的一瞬间，一名智械女性突然出现在了眼前！
E: What's going on? The moment you calibrate the target's mood, an Intellitron girl appears out of nowhere.

C: 嘘…别打岔。
E: Shh... don't interrupt!

C: 这样的抽打持续了好几分钟才停止——不是因为善心大发，而是因为他用尽了气力。
E: The whipping only stops after several minutes. No, it isn't because the trainer decides to let the cone go, but because he has exhausted all his energy.

C: 它还有救吗？
E: Can it still be salvaged?

C: 它果然一幅明星做派，压根没打算向你问好。
E: Indeed, it has an air about it and has no intention to greet you.

C: 回想起来，我在匹诺康尼…已经待了很久啊。
E: Come to think of it, I have been in Penacony... for quite a long time.

C: 能忍受孤独也不错。
E: If you can tolerate it, solitude isn't a bad idea.

C: 不，我只是一个苦苦寻找梦中情人的{RUBY_B#钟情广告牌}可怜逐梦客{RUBY_E#}罢了。
E: I am neither. I am just a {RUBY_B#Lovestruck Billboard}poor Dreamchaser{RUBY_E#} tirelessly searching for the lover of my dreams.

C: 就这样吧，拜拜。
E: That's all. Bye-bye.

C: 靠近舞台。
E: You draw closer to the stage.

C: 每一次震颤都令你的心跳随之鼓噪，你的血脉为之偾张——
E: Each tremor sets your heart pounding, your veins electrified.

C: 这样一来，不就显得为这事儿愁眉苦脸的我也像个笑话了吗？唉……
E: And now I look like a joke for being worried in the first place? *sigh*

C: 不就是没尾巴吗！我没有尾巴，难道你们就有咯？
E: It's just a tail! So what? Do any of you have tails?

C: 你看，我有故事听、他们有钱拿，这不是双赢吗？
E: Look, I get to listen to stories, and they get to earn some money, so that's a win-win all around, no?

C: 我相信它肯定抓住了我们心底的什么东西。
E: I am convinced that the city has captured something in all our hearts.

C: 「赫尔辛格大礼堂」就是我当时最得意的作品。为了完成这个「伟大」的作品，我牺牲了很多建筑本应有的东西……
E: The Herzinger Auditorium was my proudest work at the time. However, in order to create this great work, I sacrificed a lot of elements that a building was supposed to have...

C: 真见鬼，这两个家伙是怎么凑到一块儿去的？
E: How did this happen? How did these two end up together?

C: 尊敬的宾客，欢迎来到「白日梦」酒店。请问有什么可以为您效劳的吗？
E: Welcome to The Reverie, dear guest. How may I assist you?

C: 当然啦，您要是自信自己的故事足够精彩，可以被用来交换酒水…那我也求之不得。
E: And of course, if you have splendid stories to exchange for drinks... I'd be delighted to hear them.

C: 就算是为了「合群」，您也应当尝尝这种气泡糖浆。
E: I recommend trying this concoction, even if it's just to blend in.

C: 但说实话，这对我而言反倒缺乏吸引力。更何况…人们来到「{RUBY_B#匹诺康尼}梦想之城{RUBY_E#}」往往是为了追求自己想要、却在现实里得不到的东西——而我？我很满意自己现在的生活。
E: But to be honest, it doesn't appeal to me... People come to {RUBY_B#Penacony}the City of Dreams{RUBY_E#} to pursue what they cannot attain in reality. As for me, I am already content with my current life.

C: 如果您或同伴遭遇任何危险情况，请及时联系我或者附近的猎犬。
E: If you or your companions find yourselves in a dangerous situation, please reach out to me or any nearby Bloodhounds.

C: 如果您对「入梦」相关问题抱有任何疑问，请咨询入梦护理师总管乔凡娜。
E: For questions about entering the dream, please feel free to ask Giovanna, the Chief Dream Nurse.

C: …哈哈，有吗？那我可能恰巧和您的某位朋友长得像。
E: ...Haha, is that so? Perhaps I happen to look like one of your friends.

C: 飞上高空之后，我便将身体伸展成饼状，增大阻力、减少速度，最后安全落回地面。
E: When I was up in the air, I stretched myself into a pancake-like form to catch more air and eventually safely landed on the ground.

C: 幸好公司认可我们的价值，在星球表面建起了许多避雨场所。现在基本没人会消失在雨中了。
E: Fortunately, IPC has recognized our value and constructed rain shelters on the planet. As a result, disappearances during rainfall are now few and far between.

C: 唔，算了。反正我也不是真的皮皮西。看来还有很多要学啊……
E: Well, forget it. I'm not a real Pepeshi anyway. I suppose I still have much to learn...

C: 唔…上次发生了那样的事，怪不好意思的。算了吧~
E: Well... I feel a bit embarrassed after what happened last time. Let's forget it...

C: 你心情复杂地挂断了电话——这个结果谁能想到呢？
E: You end the call with mixed emotions. Who could have expected such an outcome?

C: 叮铃…叮铃…叮铃……
E: *ring*... *ring*... *ring*...

C: 在不断重复的吞吃动作中，你似乎理解了「贪饕」的命途……
E: While repeating the act of swallowing, you seem to gain an understanding of the Path of Voracity...

C: 我知道了。
E: Got it.

C: {NICKNAME}乘客…在这列车上发生过很多你不知道的事情呢帕。等到以后时机成熟了再和你讲吧！
E: {NICKNAME}... many things that have happened on this train aren't within your knowledge. I'll tell you about them when the time is right!

C: 欢迎光临，请问有什么需要的吗？
E: Welcome, how can I help you?

C: 飞碟堡，橡木蛋糕卷，「鲱鱼先生」柠檬塔……今晚吃什么好呢？
E: Floatdisc burgers, oak cake rolls, "Mr. Herring's" lemon tarts... What should I have for dinner tonight...?

C: 因为我、以及我的同事们，一直在用双手筑就全新的东西。
E: Because I, and my coworkers, are building something totally new with our own hands.

C: 真正的钟表小子？
E: The real Clockie?

C: 我们都自顾自地沉浸在自己的领域里，自顾自地想要证明自己才是正确的那一个。只不过这一切如今早已没有了意义……
E: We were both obsessed with our fields, trying hard to prove that we were the right one. However, this fight had long lost its meaning...

C: 到了最后…就连争吵的声音也消失了。我们之间仅剩沉默。
E: At the end of the day... we didn't even argue with each other anymore. What was left between us was sheer silence.

C: 既然你有兴趣，我们就来聊聊吧。
E: Since you're up for it, let's do it.

C: 哎呀，这么快，我还没聊够呢……
E: Ah, so soon? I still have so much to talk about...

C: 我可以邀请你上车。
E: I can invite you to the Express.

C: …怪怪的——滴答？这都被你看出来了，我的朋友……
E: ...Strange... Tick-tock? I see you noticed, my friend...

C: 短暂的沉默之后，「钟表小子」又出现在你的面前。
E: After a brief silence, Clockie appears before your eyes once more.

C: …体验非常地差。
E: ...I hate it here.

C: 看着原本骄傲的父亲，沦落到必须看人脸色行事的地步，我实在无法忍受这种生活的落差，赌气离开了匹诺康尼，再也没和任何人联系过。
E: Seeing my once-proud father being reduced to being subservient to others, I could no longer deal with our sudden fall from grace. So, I left Penacony selfishly and never contacted anyone again.

C: 先聊到这吧。
E: That's enough chit-chat for now.

C: 你分不清这些收藏品比起外面的同款究竟有什么特别。不过有没有可能——它们本就无关紧要呢？
E: You cannot tell just what is special about these pieces compared to the identical models outside. Although perhaps... They're not important at all?

C: 圆桌不似长桌，没有主次和对立关系，「均衡」的星神见了它高低也得鞠两个躬。
E: Unlike a long table, round tables have no sense of priority or opposition. The Aeon of Equilibrium would still have to bow twice upon seeing it.

C: 没什么除非！哼，就是这样！我们连朋友都做不了！
E: No, unless nothing! Humph, I said what I said! We can't even be friends!

C: 低沉的嗓音混杂着急促鼓点，在你的耳畔骤然响起。
E: A low voice, amidst the sharp beat of a drum, suddenly resonates in your ears.

C: 我才不会祝福你们呢。
E: I do not wish you well.

C: 现在我和您并肩坐在这里，共同度过了一段美好的时光，这就够了，不是吗？
E: Sitting side-by-side here, sharing in these beautiful moments, don't you think that's enough?

C: （进行搭讪）
E: (Chat)

C: 我的音级远高于那些普通的家人。虽然我不知道你的小把戏来自哪道命途的力量…但无论如何，这种挠痒痒似的把戏，在我身上只会被「同谐」的力量「啪」地弹开。
E: My scale degree goes far beyond the average Family member. I may not know what Path your little trick drew power from, but... that trifling ability merely bounces off of me thanks to the power of Harmony.

C: 如果现在只有她一个人，你怀疑她甚至会直接瘫下来。
E: You suspect that if she were by herself, she would be falling to the floor.

C: 若有一天，我与世界作别，请勿陈鲜花于棺柩前，请为我呈上桂冠、甲胄与宝剑……
E: If the day would come when I would bid farewell to this world, do not offer fresh blooms before my casket, but a laurel crown, a chest of armor, and a sharp sword...

C: 你在自言自语什么呢？
E: Are you talking to yourself?

C: …当然，这只是通常情况。如果是「无限夫长」阿伊里涅夫响应召唤，那有人可要吃苦头了。
E: ...Of course, that's just how it usually happens. If it's Aelenev, the Eternal Centurion, who answers the call, then someone is going to get punished.

C: 听我说，朋友们！还记得我们的梦想吗？我们要在「谐乐大典」的舞台上留下让世人永远传唱的旋律！
E: Listen up, friends! Remember our dream? We're going to rock a melody on the Charmony Festival stage that'll make the whole world sing!

C: 看到不喜欢的攻略就不看呗，非得在评论区找存在感，还向家族举报，缺不缺德啊？
E: You don't have to read a strategy if you don't like it. Why you gotta go posting on message boards, seeking attention — or snitching to The Family? It's wrong, isn't it?

C: 哎，最近挺少在流梦礁碰到他，希望那些该死的家族走狗不会为难他。
E: Ah, I barely ever run into him around the Dreamflux Reef anymore... I hope those Family dogs don't go giving him any trouble.

C: 说实话，流梦礁的生活条件虽然不怎么样，氛围可真是非常宜居了。
E: If I'm honest, the living conditions in the Dreamflux Reef may not be anything to write home about. But, the atmosphere is not bad at all.

C: 科琳皱起了眉，表情却并不夸张。
E: Colleen's face folds into a gentle frown.

C: 也许下次该让孩子们学习一些悲伤的歌曲，毕竟这是无可回避的人生话题。总有一天他们会飞向天空，而我会坠落大地——
E: Maybe next time I should teach the children some sad songs. This is a life topic that cannot be avoided forever, after all. One day, they will soar toward the sky, and I will fall to the ground...

C: 在折纸大学，忆质动力学的课本里居然连她的名字都没有，取而代之的是所谓的学术权威——她无力为自己辩解，但这并不是他们篡夺学术的借口！
E: At the Paperfold University, her name isn't even mentioned in the memoria dynamics textbook. Instead, it was replaced by the so-called academic authorities — she cannot defend herself, but this is no excuse for their usurpation of her works!

C: 虽然「钟表匠」先生从未亲口承认，但我相信，钟表小子的故事就是他自己的故事！也就是我们匹诺康尼的故事！
E: Mr. Watchmaker might not have admitted it, but I believe Clockie's stories are his own! In other words, they are the stories of us Penaconians!

C: 听说最近有人用它的形象推销自己的产品，甚至在未经原作者许可的情况下扭曲钟表小子的设定，太可恶了！
E: I heard that someone has been using his image to promote their products lately, even modifying the original settings of Clockie without his creator's permission! This is abhorrent!

C: 自从我成年以来，我便一直在为钟表小子博物馆的建议而奔走。
E: Ever since I came of age, I've been actively advocating for the Clockie Museum proposal.

C: 你活了这么久吗？
E: Have you lived for such a long time?

C: 你见过匹诺康尼现实的星空吗？许多年前，我们还在战火里的时候，它们就挂在那儿。我们坐在一片废墟里望着它们，它们也望着我们。
E: Have you seen the real stars of Penacony firsthand? Ages ago, while we were still at war, they adorned the sky above us. We sat in the ruins and gazed upward at them as they looked down upon us.

C: 因为这些都是历史，它们早已过去，留给我的只有这片深邃的长梦。
E: Because it's all history. They are bygone days, leaving me with only this deep and long dream.

C: 再见，贫穷的客人——下次记得带着硬币来。
E: Goodbye, impoverished guest. Bring me some coins next time.
Tweet!?

C: 还有钟表小子，别看他两条腿短短的，喝下苏乐达之后，跑起来可没几只鸟能追上他啾。
E: And Clockie too. Don't be fooled by his short legs. Once he gulps down SoulGlad, few birds can keep up with his speed, tweet.

C: 嘿呀！是魔法硬币！
E: Hi-yah! It's a magic coin!

C: 让我想想…对了，有个很会跳舞的孩子，非要缠着人偶朋友们和它比比，<color=#dbc291ff>走廊里的几位舞者</color>对它都很友善啾～
E: Let me think... Oh, that's right, there's a child that's adept at dancing, and incessantly challenges its puppet friends. <color=#dbc291ff>The dancers along the hallway</color> are very friendly to it, tweet!

C: 但实践证明，银河化战略是成功的道路，也是必须坚持的策略。
E: But the reality has proven that a cosmic strategy is the successful solution, and one in which that we must persist.

C: 我的生产地是阿斯蒙星环，那地方在追逐「欢愉」中走了条偏路，所有人都寻求疯狂的感官刺激，以此渴求乐子神的瞥视。
E: I was manufactured in the Asmon Rim, a planet that took a wrong turn in their pursuit of the Elation. Everyone sought heightened stimulation of the senses, all craving for a mere glimpse from the Laughter.

C: 如果用现实中的时间来计数…我已经很老了，{NICKNAME}。
E: If we were to calculate using real-time... I'm already very old, {NICKNAME}.

C: 多么伟大的英雄，却在自己的领土上无人问津，甚至被后人解读为无谋的暴君，何其可悲！
E: Such a great hero, yet unsung in his own territory. He was even labeled a witless tyrant by later generations. The tragedy!

C: 不过…你如果想买下它的话，要准备好「代价」哦。
E: But... if you wish to buy it, you must be prepared to pay the "price."

C: 我也觉得我很美！
E: I find myself gorgeous, too!

C: 我曾拜访过某个阴郁的墓场，那里每座墓碑旁都有连接着铰链的钟塔，我逐个鸣钟，想知道究竟是否有人被过早埋葬；
E: I once visited a gloomy graveyard. Every tombstone in that graveyard was linked to a hinged bell. I rang each bell one by one, wanting to know whether anyone had been prematurely buried.

C: 你们也上船了？什么时候…好吧，那看好你二哥，顺便帮我探探他的口风……
E: You've boarded the ship as well? When did you... Alright. In that case, look after your second-eldest brother and try to find out more information from him.

C: 在表达基础需求之外，又额外演化出一套如此复杂繁琐的交流体系…或许人们愿称之为文明的进步、文化的丰饶，但我只觉得聒噪。
E: The evolution of such a complicated and tedious communication system on top of merely expressing one's basic demands... Perhaps humans view this as the progress of civilization and the abundance of culture. Yet, I find such a system truly grating.

C: 第一次见到他的时候，几个皮皮西人几乎要和他动起手来了。
E: The first time I met him, he was about to throw hands with several Pepeshi.

C: 嗨，你也是为了雅各布大厨来的吗？
E: Hi, are you here to see Chef Jacob too?

C: 只有当你远离母星，才会想起它的好。所以，我要为这段演绎增加一个小小的情景。
E: You only appreciate your home planet once you're away. With that in mind, I'll add a scenario to the enactment.

C: 实在不好意思…这是我头一次出远门。我还…不太熟练。
E: I'm sorry... It's my first time traveling alone. Th—This is all a bit new to me.

C: 嗯…虽然我也不知道，这算不算是毫无必要的担心——但离开工作岗位这么久，我总觉得有点…害怕。
E: Hmm... I'm not sure if my concerns are justified, but being away from work for this long, I can't help feeling a bit anxious.

C: 你知道吗？这个世界上最恶心的事，并不是有罪的人逍遥法外——而是看到自己的客户畏手畏脚，活得像个受气包！
E: You know what? The worst feeling in the world isn't watching criminals get off scot-free, but seeing your client living their life gripped by fear, becoming no different from a doormat!

C: 我很好奇…
E: I'm very curious...

C: 我没什么想问的了。
E: I don't have any more questions.

C: 还有还有，人家还记得他有句头口禅，好像是什么「请叫我美梦小镇的『财富园丁』，嚯嗬嗬嗬……」
E: Oh yes, oh yes. I also remember that he has a catchphrase that goes something like this: "Please, call me Dreamville's Wealthy Gardener, hehehe...".

C: 啾——客官的手好温暖啾~请再多摸摸人家的羽毛啾~
E: Tweet — your hand is so warm, tweet~ Please stroke my feathers more often, tweet~

C: 啾！是魔法硬币啾！
E: Tweet! A magic coin, tweet!

C: …船长周边预售已开启 ，为什么周边这么火爆，还不是因为船长太迷人！咳咳，这个现任船长嘛，正是在下！所以……
E: ...The pre-sale for the captain's merchandise has started. Why is the merchandise so popular? It's because the captain is too charming! *cough*... Well, the current captain... That's me! Soooo...

C: …船长周边预售已开启 ，为什么周边这么火爆，还不是因为船长太迷人！咳咳，这个现任船长嘛，正是在下！所以……
E: ...The pre-sale for the captain's merchandise has started. Why is the merchandise so popular? It's because the captain is too charming! *cough*... Well, the current captain... That's me! Soooo...

C: 是的，汪。能上空艇的都是最尊贵的客人，服务水平也要水涨船高，汪。
E: It is, woof. Only the most distinguished guests get to board this airship, so the service we render has to be elevated too, woof.

C: 你有什么推荐的饮料吗？
E: Do you have any drinks to recommend?

C: 你尝试用钟表把戏触碰宠勿饮的内心，但无论表盘如何转动，你发现它都不为所动。
E: You attempt to rouse emotions from Soda Pop Pup with Clockwork, but no matter how much the dial moves, you realize that it remains unfazed.

C: 请您放心，我已经以住客的视角完整体验过空艇上的所有设施，所以我很清楚目前住客们的需求。
E: Rest assured that I've already experienced all the facilities on this airship from a guest's perspective, so I daresay I have an excellent understanding of their current needs.

C: 新招的这批船员，他们的过往经验与技能看起来和招聘需求并不匹配。但我认为，每个生物都有其独特的潜力与价值，通过传统的途径是无法衡量的。
E: While the professional experience and abilities of the newly recruited crew members don't seem to meet the hiring criteria, it is my belief that every living thing has their own unique potential and value. These things can't be measured through traditional means.

C: 适当的自由与足够的理解，将最大可能地挖掘出他们的潜力。太过严苛的管理反而有损船长的声誉。
E: Giving them adequate freedom and ample understanding unlocks their highest potential. Micromanaging them, on the other hand, will only end up sullying the captain's reputation.

C: 你刚说什么？大点声——
E: What did you just say? Speak louder —

C: 「哟，看看这两张票…」
E: "Oh, I have two tickets with me..."

C: 说说谐乐学院？
E: Tell me about Charmony Academy?

C: 是得下狠手。
E: He needs a good whoopin'.

C: 我赞同阿锡卡教授的看法。无论外观如何繁杂，最基础的功能性都不可或缺。
E: I agree with Professor Asika's views. It does not matter how elaborate the exterior appearance is. A construction must at least serve its most basic functions.

C: 可现实就是如此无奈：有的人能凭资历尸位素餐，霸占教授的位置；而有的人满肚子真才实学，却只能开个选修课。
E: But, there is no changing the reality: Some people manage to occupy the professor positions based purely on their history and experience, while those who possess genuine talent and intellect are left with nothing but elective courses.

C: 参加博识学会讲座的时候，就听说有位教授对白痴过敏…如今我可算知道那究竟是个什么滋味了！
E: When I attended a lecture at the Intelligentsia Guild, I heard that there was a professor who was allergic to stupidity... Now I know what that feels like!

C: 少女的眼睛闪闪发亮，让人不由想起学校中央永不熄灭的灯光。
E: The young girl's eyes sparkle with a light reminiscent of the undying flames of the campus lamps.

C: 我们喜剧社成立也有点年头了，本来只是小众社团，制作梦泡的初衷也只是想留个纪念，给社团挣点外快维持运转用的。
E: Our comedy club has been around for a few years now. We were small at first. Our original inspiration for making dream bubbles was just to leave some kind of memento and help earn some extra cash to maintain operations.

C: 不知道你有没有看过我们喜剧社的梦泡，有条件的话，我还是建议你来现场看，和梦泡里完全是两回事。
E: I don't know if you've seen any of our club's dream bubbles? If you can, I would recommend that you come and see us live — it's a completely different experience.

C: 以前从场地到演出，所有环节都要我们自己掏钱，光是租金就要一大笔钱，光靠卖门票的那点收入哪里够呀！
E: Back then, from the venue to the performance, everything was paid for out of our own pockets. Just the rent alone took up a huge chunk, and the ticket sales were nowhere enough to cover it!

C: 是向学校申请过的。再说，乐器上绘满了忍符，除了我们应该也没人会使用了。
E: We requested them from the school. Also, the instruments have been scribbled full of ninja talismans — no one else but us would use them.

C: 看来先前的演出经历在乐队成员心中留下了极为深刻的印象，以至于任何情绪都无法动摇这份热情。这就是音乐的力量吧。
E: Looks like the performances left an indelible, lasting impression in the band's hearts — no amount of emotion can cause this passion to waver. This is the power of music.

C: 是啊，时代是变了，我熟悉的音乐风格渐渐都见不到了，而我擅长的那些技法和规范，也成了你们口中老旧的学院派。
E: Yes, times have changed. The music styles I'm familiar with have slowly become irrelevant, and the techniques and standards I pride myself in are now also the dated concepts students gripe about.

C: 哎哟，具体是什么事我忘了，但…总之…总之就是非常感谢
E: Oof, I can't remember what it was exactly, but... anyway... I'm truly grateful!

C: …至少现在，我只能这么想。
E: ...At least I can only hope so for now.

C: 但…那个人会是谁呢…{NICKNAME}同学，会是你吗？
E: But... who could that person be... {NICKNAME}, might you be the one?

C: 在「寰宇蝗灾」的时代，众神争斗不休。有一天，「存护」克里珀找到「繁育」塔伊兹育罗斯，对祂说：「我知道你是一位很要强的星神，但从今天开始，你不必再要强了。」
E: During the era of the Swarm Disaster, the gods were in constant conflict. One day, Qlipoth the Preservation found Tayzzyronth the Propagation and said to THEM, "I know you try very hard to be popular, but from this day forth, you no longer need to do so."

C: 有什么感兴趣的目的地？
E: Any destinations you're interested in?

C: 但很遗憾，我无法解答你的疑惑。同为忆者，我不能擅自打听他人的行动。
E: But sadly, I can't indulge your curiosity. As Memokeepers, we can't pry into each other's plans.

C: {F#她}{M#他}还会再来，直到我也成为{F#她}{M#他}记忆的一部分…对吗？
E: Will {F#she}{M#he} keep visiting me until I find my place in {F#her}{M#his} memory?

C: 呵呵。虽说有所涉猎，但我可称不上通晓占卜，也不觉得存在确信的未来。
E: Hee-hee. I have dabbled in such things, but it's not like I'm well-versed in divination, nor do I believe in a predetermined future.

C: 即使隔着屏幕，透过如雷的掌声，你也能感受到另一侧观众们的激动和亢奋。
E: Even through the screen and the thunderous applause, you can feel the excitement and stimulation of the audience on the other side.

C: 看累了，不看了。
E: My eyes are tired. I'm done looking.

C: 我都说了，这里什么梦都有…
E: I told you, there's every kind of dream in here...

C: 是啊是啊，那个阴阳怪气的家伙！我敢说，整个筑材物流部都没有比他更恶心的人了！
E: Yes, yes — that sarcastic guy! I dare say there isn't anyone more annoying than him in the entire Building Material Logistics Department!

C: 呃…有零卡苏乐达吗？
E: Er... Is there a Zero-Sugar SoulGlad?

C: 你就这么喜欢恐怖片？
E: You're THAT into horror movies?

C: 别烦老大。
E: Don't mess with the boss.

C: 受影响的物品会漂浮…
E: All the affected items are floating around...

C: 得想个办法把它固定住。
E: I need to hold them in place somehow.

C: 近期的电影节、音乐会想不想了解一下？
E: Or perhaps you want to know about upcoming movie festivals and concerts?

C: 我…这不是车多吗，等我再观察观察……
E: Th—There's so much traffic here... Let me wait and see...

C: 你说这么大的城市，怎么就连个红绿灯都没有，也太不安全了吧？
E: It's so unsafe. Such a big city, and yet not even a traffic light, don't you think?

C: 有俩座位靠近厕所，一开始没人愿意占，我手快给抢到了。
E: There were two seats near the restroom that no one initially wanted, so I quickly grabbed them.

C: 世人只得见它披挂的时讯，你却偏摸索它跼蹐的灵魂；
E: The world knows it for its garments, its headlines bold, but you seek its essence, its stories untold.

C: …鉴于美梦剧团近来频频遭袭，我认为家族有必要出台「梦境造物保护法」，你觉得呢……
E: ...Considering the recent attacks on the Sweet Dreams Troupe, I think The Family should introduce a protection act for the Dreamscape creations... What do you think?

C: 时间就是金钱。
E: Time is money.

C: 随时为您服务。
E: I'm at your service anytime.

C: 说好的保我进决赛，结果一上台就给刷下来了，还被那几个评委当众羞辱。
E: I was guaranteed to get through to the finals, but I was eliminated as soon as I got on the stage. I was even ridiculed by those judges.

C: 合同上写得很清楚，我们负责收集比赛信息，提供训练方案，具体名次由你的努力和实力决定。
E: It's all written in the agreement: We're responsible for collecting competition information and providing training solutions. Your hard work and talent determine how you're placed.

C: 好多年没见过这么刺激的盛会海选了。
E: I haven't seen such an exciting Festivity Audition in years!

C: 此外，嗅到商机的苏乐达将赞助额提到了历年来的最高点。随之而来的，是住宿、餐饮、奢侈品等多个领域的营收抬升。
E: Also, SoulGlad — having sniffed out a business opportunity — has increased their sponsorship budget to the highest it's been historically. Subsequently, the revenues from accommodation, food and drinks, luxury goods, and other industries have also followed suit.

C: 选手们摩拳擦掌，火花一触即发…
E: The contestants are raring to go, and sparks are about to fly...

C: 现在想想我真是幸运，或许这就是命吧，有时候啊，你不得不信这玩意儿……
E: I lucked out, come to think of it. That's life, I suppose. Sometimes, it's hard not to believe in such things...

C: 那我就在等着吧，唯有等待……
E: Then I shall keep waiting — I can only wait...

C: 我不信任这些公司来的人，他们的眼里只有利益。
E: I don't trust those IPC people. They only have profit in their eyes.

C: 这是知更鸟小姐亲自嘱托下来的，家主那边也首肯的，你不信可以找人确认下。
E: This was Miss Robin's personal instructions, and the family head also approved it first thing. Go confirm it with someone else if you don't believe me.

C: 我…我恐高……
E: I... I'm afraid of heights...

C: 可不能出问题…
E: Nothing can go wrong...

C: 我们来一局吧。
E: Let's have a match.

C: 摊位售卖抽选盲盒，获得隐藏款的概率是万分之三，前来光顾的客人络绎不绝。
E: The stall sells lottery lootboxes, with the probability of getting the hidden version at <unbreak>0.03%</unbreak>. In spite of this, customers still come in droves.

C: 「咔哒」，天亮请睁眼。
E: "Click," open your eyes as it gets bright.

C: 没什么想玩的…
E: Not much you want to play...

C: 你的目光扫过收藏架上的藏品，最后停留在「晖长石号 / 开拓之尾号 / 塔塔洛夫号 / 飞翔时针号模型」上。
E: As you cast your gaze across the collectibles on the display shelf, it lands on the "The Radiant Feldspar / The Trailblaze's Stern / The Tatalov / The Soaring Clock Hand model."

C: 离开
E: Leave

C: 大地兽阿斯忒利翁
E: Let's talk about Asterion.

C: 可承住我理想的创作，并非如今大家认可我的原因…人们只记得我常为奥赫玛的名流作画，也只爱我笔下的黄金裔。
E: But people do not currently acknowledge me due to the creations that bear my true dream... People remember me mostly for my paintings of the upper echelon of Okhema and my portrayal of the Chrysos Heirs.

C: 但朱诺婶婶，你也听我一句劝吧：别再试图干涉我的生活了。
E: I've got a piece of advice for you too, Aunt Juno. Stay. Out. Of. My. Business.

C: 她让我学会了很多，尤其是…「美」。这种事物绝非仅限于眼之所见，而是需要用心去感受。
E: She's taught me a lot, especially about... beauty. It's not just about pleasing the eye, but about nourishing the soul.

C: 通常来说，{NICKNAME}不会这么快理解现状的。但是既然你这么说了，我愿意相信我自己，我们应该分别是本我，自我，超我。
E: Usually, {NICKNAME} wouldn't understand the situation so quickly. But since you said it, I'm willing to believe in myself. We should be the id, ego, and superego.

C: 这么多问题你就让我问一个啊？
E: So many questions, and I only get to ask one?

C: 退化到空中生活。
E: Regress to a life in the sky.

C: 退化到水中生活。
E: Regress to a life in the water.

C: 重置位置。
E: Reset Position

C: 你抓起一把石榴籽塞了满口——真的假的？！果核竟然也能吃，而且口感还和仙人快乐茶里的珍珠如此相似！
E: You scoop up a handful of those seeds and pop them in your mouth... Really?! Even the cores are edible, boasting a texture akin to the sticky rice balls found in Immortal's Delight.

C: 达利安！别在那鬼扯了！锋狼都溜进来了！
E: Stop talking nonsense over there, Dorian! The direwolves are coming in!

C: 但他们说的，不一样。有人说，水果在餐前吃更好，有人说，之后吃更好。
E: But what they said was confusing. Some said to eat fruit before meals, while others said after.

C: 您可以随意出入浴场、免费使用浴场的各项设施，同时，在需额外付费的项目中享有专属折扣，但一部分贩卖品不在此限。
E: You can enter and exit the bath anytime, use the bath's various facilities free of charge, and enjoy exclusive discounts on paid amenities, though this offer does not extend to some products.

C: 我想买点什么。
E: I want to buy something.

C: 你怎么不说话？
E: Say something!

C: 你到底经历了什么？
E: What happened to you?

C: 能再和我讲讲你的经历吗？
E: Can you tell me about what you experienced again?

C: 听闻人越多，佳酿的口味就越醇厚。所以…在奥赫玛，没有人会独自痛饮，我们总是习惯了一起面对困难，一起庆祝胜利。
E: I hear that the flavor of this fine wine grows richer the more people there are. So... no one drinks alone in Okhema. We prefer to face life's difficulties together, just as we celebrate our victories together.

C: 咳咳咳——各位，听我说，我们奥赫玛的繁荣离不开每一位居民的建设！每一位新生力量都至关重要，所以，我们得拿出来热情来欢迎我们的新朋友，加入奥赫玛！
E: Ahem — Everyone, hear my word! It is thanks to the hard work of its citizens that Okhema has come to be so prosperous! Every new strength that comes to be is crucial to our success. This is why we must show enthusiasm as we welcome our new friends to Okhema!

C: 而如果因为种种原因，你连那一点「自己的事」都没得做…能确认自己还活着的方法，就只剩下了这样远远地看着别人。
E: And if for whatever reason you can't even get those personal affairs done... the only way to confirm that you're still alive is to stand at a distance like this, watching others.

C: 啊，这个呀。
E: Ah, about that.

C: 为了你的安全起见，现在还是眼看手勿动吧。
E: Out of consideration for your safety, please look but don't touch.

C: 我不小心触发了对话。
E: I accidentally triggered this conversation.

C: 好，我帮你看着东西。
E: Alright, I'll look after your things.

C: 您可知道，除去污渍最好的工具是什么？每个人的见解不同，而我的答案是——「一切」。
E: Do you know what the best tool is for removing stains? People have different views on this, but my answer is... "Anything."

C: 作为战士，我本该杀死那些怪物、为同胞们复仇…但我没有，我逃跑了。许多年轻战士因此骂我是懦夫，认为我应该和同伴们一块战死。
E: As a warrior, I should have killed those monsters and taken vengeance for my fellows... But I didn't. I ran away. Many young soldiers call me a coward for this. They believe that I should have died alongside my comrades.

C: 通过考核的新兵会首先接受体能、武艺和阵型的训练，从门关月一直持续到收获月。在随后的纷争月中，那些表现优异者会被编入军队出战。
E: Recruits that pass examinations will receive physical training, martial arts training, and formation training from the Month of Gate until the Month of Reaping. In the following Month of Strife, those who performed outstandingly will be entered into the army to fight.

C: 而且这是同时考验三人的试炼，你的同伴在哪里？
E: And this is a trial for groups of three. Where are your companions?

C: 宝贝儿…带秘酿来…不管聊天还是别的…我都陪你……
E: My dear... Bring ambrosia... Chat or otherwise... I'll accompany you...

C: 你明明知道我在等你！
E: You knew I was waiting for you!

C: 若无他事，异邦勇士，我将继续工作。
E: Continue my work, I shall, if nothing else there is, warrior of the outlands.

C: 两者有什么区别吗？
E: What's the difference?

C: 你捧起瓶身，一饮而尽。秘酿强烈的风味如一场风暴，连泰坦也会被放倒——
E: You raise the bottle and down it into your hatch. The ambrosia's intense flavor hits you like a storm, capable of toppling even a Titan...

C: 塔拉萨波利斯，对吗？你和另一位朋友用这个杜撰的地名乔装成异邦人。
E: Thalassa's Thalassopolis, is that right? You and your friend used this fake location name and pretended to be travelers.

C: 嗯…我扪心自问，还是放不下对缇宝大人的复杂心绪。也许今后会有机会与她们敞开心扉说说话……
E: Yes... If I were honest with myself, I still can't relinquish my complicated feelings towards Lady Tribbie. Maybe after today, there might come a chance to talk with them about what happened...

C: 但至少现在的我，还做不到。
E: But for now, I am unable to do so.

C: 奥赫玛的卫士，基本都是山之民。我们，敬业。
E: Most guards, in Okhema, Mountain Dwellers. We dedicated.

C: 也有，怪胎。揍一顿，会好些。
E: Some odd. Beating them helps.

C: 据说，吉奥里亚的子民，不会见到塞纳托斯，只会回归大地。
E: Georios' kin, do not meet Thanatos. Simply return to the earth.

C: 相逢即是雅努斯的祝福，身后这幅壁画是我的作品，不知你对壁画是否感兴趣——我想听听你的意见。
E: Our chance encounter must be blessed by Janus. This mural behind me is my creation. I'm curious if you have an interest in murals — I'd love to hear your thoughts.

C: 哦，你要走了吗？好吧...谢谢你的倾听。
E: Oh, are you leaving? Well... thanks for listening.

C: 虽然咱们奥赫玛没有他们说的那种铁链，但确实有不少道路都架设在云端。
E: Although we Okhemans don't have iron chains like that, a lot of our roads are built high in the clouds.

C: 眼前的男人鬼鬼祟祟躲在屏风后，念念有词地望着远处的浴池，却迟迟不肯下水。
E: The man before you is sneakily hiding behind the screen, mumbling something while staring at the distant bath, yet he hesitates to get in.

C: 那消息该不会是你…
E: Ah, that message wasn't from you, was it...

C: 恶人先告状是吧？
E: Oh, playing the victim first, are we?

C: 实不相瞒，我是这云石天宫的壁画画师，最近灵感枯竭，但此物让我重拾了创作的动力…你应该不介意我照着它画点什么吧？
E: To be honest, I am a mural artist for this Marmoreal Palace. Recently, I've been struggling with a lack of inspiration, but this object has reignited my creative drive... I hope you wouldn't mind if I used it as a reference for my work?

C: 就不能好好工作吗？
E: Can't you just work properly?

C: …故乡么。或许有吧，但在我看来，那并非是个需要思念的地方，更像是在寻找。
E: ...My home, hmm? Perhaps, but the way I see it, it isn't a place that needs to be thought about, rather to seek out.

C: 若是一味地追求丰产而不停耕种，却疏于对土地加以养护，那么迟早有一天，我们赖以生存的根基就会被榨干。
E: If you simply pursue abundant harvests and spend all your time reaping without nurturing the land, one day the very foundation of our survival will dry up and wither.

C: 自分别起，我已经很久没见过其余同伴了。神驹…难道就只剩我了吗？
E: It's been an age since we separated, and I saw them last. O, divine foals... Am I really the only one left?

C: 查看《小助理的杰作》
E: View Assistant's Masterpiece

C: 我这人你也知道，对朋友的要求很高。身份、外貌、职业、谈吐…一样都不能差。
E: You know me, I have high standards for friends. Status, appearance, profession, eloquence... I don't compromise.

C: 无妨，孩子，泰坦会宽恕你的无心之失。那么现在，请将项链交给我吧，让我来作出判断，它是否有资格陈列在女神们洁净的供台上。
E: Fear not, child, the Titans will overlook your innocent mistake. Now, hand over the necklace to me and I shall judge whether it is fit to be placed upon the pure altar of The Fates.

C: 那么，阁下，我还是那句话：天底下并无免费的神谕。愿{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}揭示你的路径。
E: Very well, My Lord. Then I must remind you: There is no such thing as a free prophecy in this world. May the {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#} guide your path.

C: 阁下，有幸再度相见。上回你给我的珍宝都已纳与命运泰坦们了，不知阁下找我又有何事？
E: My Lord, it is an honor to see you again. The treasure that you bestowed to me before has been accepted by the Fate Titans. Is there something that I can help you with?

C: 哎呀，终于有人问这个了，大家都只喜欢吃，一个个的都懒得做。其实很简单的，就是要用心些，来，我教你，可记好咯。
E: Ah, finally someone wants to learn this! Everyone just loves eating, but no one wants to bother making it. It's quite simple — you just need to put in some effort. Come on. I'll teach you, so pay attention.

C: 哎，你这孩子，可太客气了。要是觉得累了，可以随时过来坐坐。
E: Ah, you're too kind. If you ever need a break, feel free to come over and talk to me anytime.

C: 你好，看你的样子，是外地来的朋友吗？我叫达娜叶，这一带我挺熟的，需要我带你到处参观一下吗？
E: Hello, you seem to be from out of town. My name's Danae, and I know this area well. Do you need a guide?

C: 好，路上小心哦。要是在这儿遇到了麻烦，需要帮助什么的，记得来找我～
E: Alright, take care. If you run into trouble and need help, remember to look me up.

C: 很遗憾…我只是个卫兵，并没有踏入内殿的权限……
E: Regrettably... I'm just a guard, and I don't have the privilege to enter the inner sanctum...

C: *我们*不知道，故乡遭受的苦难究竟是*我们*的错，还是暗中酝酿已久的祸根，也不知道究竟该对故乡抱持什么样的情感……
E: We don't know if the suffering of our homeland was our fault or if it was a calamity brewing in secret for a long time. Nor do we know what kind of emotions we should harbor toward our homeland...

C: 嗯，小灰说得对…偶尔回想过去，是为了从珍贵的人那里得到力量。可要是过分沉溺于往事…就看不清前路了。
E: Mm, Little Gray, you're right... Looking back on the past every now and then is meant to draw strength from those one holds dear. But if one indulges too much in the past... one will lose sight of the path ahead.

C: 嗷呜——累死了。这就是你喜欢的类型吧，满意了嗷呜？
E: Awooo— this is exhausting. Is this the type you like? Are you satisfied, awooo?

C: 别了，阁下。愿我们…在新世界再会。
E: Farewell, then. May we... reunite in the new world.

C: 对吧？我喜欢挥剑时那种心无旁骛、只为将对手击倒的感觉。
E: Right? I love that feeling of being completely focused while swinging a sword, thinking of nothing but defeating the opponent before you.

C: 阿格莱雅曾说…逐火是不断失却的旅途。早在千年之前，同样有一批伟大的黄金裔，歃血为盟、共同踏上击落泰坦的征程。
E: Aglaea once said... the Flame-Chase is a journey of constant loss. A thousand years ago, another group of great Chrysos Heirs forged a blood pact and embarked on a journey to strike down the Titans.

C: 哈哈，做好事、做坏事都不留名，是吧？
E: Hah! So you do good deeds or bad ones, all while staying incognito?

C: 元老院，不都是坏人吗？
E: The Council of Elders... Aren't they all bad guys?

C: 我知道你有些顾忌，这么和你说吧——我是个「暴发户」，从下面上来的那种。
E: Let me put it this way — I'm what people refer to as "nouveau riche," someone who made it big from the ground up.

C: 是这样，我呢，名叫忒斯庇斯·米里奥多卢，是一名来自米里奥斯的剧作家。
E: Well, let me introduce myself. I'm Thespis Mylonopoulos, a playwright from Milios.
